items.title: ShotPlan: Cinematic Video Generation with Learnable Planning Token
items.details: Su Guo, Guangce Liu, Haosen Yang, Jiepeng Wang et al. · 2 HF upvotes
items.summary: ShotPlan introduces a novel framework for cinematic video generation by leveraging learnable planning tokens that enhance shot-level transition management within a video diffusion model. This approach incorporates Fractional Temporal Rotary Position Embedding (FRoPE) to facilitate frame-level modeling of shot transitions, resulting in improved inter-shot consistency and flexible shot management. Experimental results indicate that ShotPlan significantly outperforms existing methods in cinematic video generation, although specific performance metrics are not provided.
items.judge: The paper introduces a framework for cinematic video generation, aligning closely with generative video models.

items.title: HarmoHOI: Harmonizing Appearance and 3D Motion for Multi-view Hand-Object Interaction Synthesis
items.details: Lingwei Dang, Juntong Li, Zonghan Li, Hongwen Zhang et al. · 8 HF upvotes
items.summary: HarmoHOI introduces a unified diffusion framework for synthesizing multi-view hand-object interaction (HOI) videos with globally aligned 3D point tracks, significantly improving multi-view consistency by co-modeling RGB videos and 3D geometry. The Mixture of Multi-view Diffusion Transformer minimizes the domain gap between 2D and 3D representations, while the Global Motion Aligning Diffusion refines point tracks into metric-scale trajectories. Experimental results demonstrate state-of-the-art performance in visual quality, motion plausibility, and geometric consistency, addressing challenges in HOI synthesis effectively.
items.judge: The paper presents a framework for synthesizing multi-view hand-object interactions, relevant to video generation.

items.title: Apple-π: Benchmarking Thinking with Video Towards Law-Grounded Physical Intelligence
items.details: Runmao Yao, Kairui Hu, Yukang Cao, Ruisi Wang et al. · 36 HF upvotes
items.summary: Apple-π introduces a novel benchmark for evaluating video generation models based on their adherence to physical laws, comprising a dataset of 400 videos across ten classical mechanics tasks, a three-stage evaluation protocol (Perception, Formulation, Deduction), and a hybrid scoring system that combines subjective and objective measures. Benchmarking 11 models reveals that the best-performing model only achieves a score of 0.473, highlighting significant gaps in law-grounded reasoning and exposing critical bottlenecks in the model's processing stages. This framework aims to guide the development of future video models toward achieving reliable physical intelligence.
items.judge: This work introduces a benchmark for evaluating video models based on physical laws, relevant to generative media.

items.title: DiFA: Inference-Time Forward-Process Alignment for Diffusion Models
items.details: Shigui Li, Delu Zeng · 2 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces DiFA (Inference-Time Forward-Process Alignment), a training-free framework that enhances diffusion model inference by treating iterative predictions as correlated observations, akin to Kalman filtering. This approach improves generative fidelity on CIFAR-10 and ImageNet, achieving significant gains in metrics such as FID and IS, by aligning inference with the forward statistical structure and incorporating a deviation guidance mechanism to maintain detail.
items.judge: Proposes a novel inference framework for diffusion models relevant to generative media.

items.title: FlowMimic: Mask-free Visual Editing and Generation with Pixel-pair Warped Flow Field for Online Video Editing Data Generation and Modality Mimicry
items.details: Dingyun Zhang, Lixue Gong, Wei Liu · 20 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces FlowMimic, a novel model that enables mask-free video editing and generation by leveraging a pixel-pair temporal warped flow field, allowing real-time generation of video editing samples from image editing data. This approach eliminates the need for labor-intensive data collection methods and enhances task scalability, achieving improved alignment between image and video modalities through modality mimic generation and editing losses. The model also incorporates sense-related tasks to internalize the comprehension of editing instructions without relying on external aids, significantly advancing the capabilities of generative video editing.
items.judge: The paper explores video editing and generation capabilities, aligning with generative media.

items.title: HOMIE: Human-object Centric Video Personalization via Multimodal Intelligent Enchancement
items.details: Yiyang Cai, Nan Chen, Rongchang Xie, Junwen Pan et al. · 46 HF upvotes
items.summary: HOMIE introduces a novel framework for human-object centric video personalization (HOCVP) that effectively addresses the limitations of inter- and intra-subject personalization by integrating global multimodal guidance within self-attention mechanisms. This approach enhances the alignment of MLLM-derived semantic features with VAE tokens while maintaining controllability and avoiding costly re-alignment, resulting in state-of-the-art performance across various HOCVP tasks. The framework's design choices, including modality-reference embedding, facilitate improved subject fidelity and interaction accuracy, particularly with abstract object representations.
items.judge: Focuses on human-object centric video personalization, relevant to video generation.

items.title: EvolvingWorld: An Open-Schema Framework for Co-Evolving Role-Play Agents and World Model in Interactive Literary World
items.details: Qing Zong, Yue Guo, Mengxin Yang, Yiwen Guo et al. · 64 HF upvotes
items.summary: EvolvingWorld introduces a novel open-schema framework for co-evolving role-play agents and world models in interactive literary environments, significantly enhancing long-horizon simulation by maintaining coherent character and world development. The framework features a Character Agent for multi-character role-play and an LLM-based World Model, enabling dynamic updates of character and world states over time. Evaluations demonstrate improved performance across 10 dimensions and 20 metrics, supported by a dataset of 138,596 training samples derived from 57 literary works.
items.judge: Introduces a framework for character and world co-evolution, relevant to world models.

items.title: ReflectWorld-MM: An Entity-Oriented Multimodal Memory System for Open-Ended Video Streams
items.details: Xiaokang Ma, Yifan Sun, Zhihong Jin, Jie Gu et al. · 23 HF upvotes
items.summary: ReflectWorld-MM introduces an entity-oriented multimodal memory system designed for open-ended video streams, significantly enhancing the ability to track and reason about persistent entities over time. It features a perception front-end for entity-resolved observations, a hierarchical long-term memory structure based on human memory theory, and a practical implementation for real-world applications. The system outperforms existing memory agents across six long-video and lifelong-memory benchmarks, achieving the highest accuracy in all cases.
items.judge: Explores memory systems for video streams, relevant to world models.

items.title: VideoRAE: Taming Video Foundation Models for Generative Modeling via Representation Autoencoders
items.details: Zhihao Xie, Junfeng Wu, Xinting Hu, Junchao Huang et al. · 7 HF upvotes
items.summary: VideoRAE introduces a novel representation autoencoder that transforms frozen video foundation model (VFM) representations into compact, generation-friendly video latents, achieving state-of-the-art class-to-video generative frame video distances (gFVDs) of 40 and 93 on UCF-101 with autoregressive and diffusion transformers, respectively. The model converges approximately 5x faster than existing autoencoder baselines and employs a local-and-global representation alignment objective to enhance semantic preservation without the need for KL regularization. The implementation will be available at the provided GitHub link.
items.judge: This paper discusses generative video models and their representation learning, aligning with primary focus areas.

items.title: Audio-Visual Flamingo: Open Audio-Visual Intelligence for Long and Complex Videos
items.details: Sreyan Ghosh, Arushi Goel, Kaousheik Jayakumar, Lasha Koroshinadze et al. · 5 HF upvotes
items.summary: Audio-Visual Flamingo (AV-Flamingo) introduces a fully open audio-visual large language model designed for understanding and reasoning over long and complex videos, significantly improving upon previous models that focused on short clips. Key innovations include a large-scale dataset with ~7M training instances emphasizing temporal and cross-modal reasoning, a three-stage curriculum for progressive training, and a Temporal Audio-Visual Interleaved Chain-of-Thought framework that enhances temporal alignment and interpretability. AV-Flamingo demonstrates superior performance across 15+ benchmarks, outperforming similarly sized models and competing effectively with larger models, particularly in real-world audio-visual tasks.
items.judge: The paper presents a state-of-the-art audio-visual model for understanding long videos, aligning with generative video models.

items.title: DSWorld: A Data Science World Model for Efficient Autonomous Agents
items.details: Zherui Yang, Fan Liu, Hao Liu · 4 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces DSWorld, a novel framework for autonomous data science agents that enhances efficiency by predicting environment state transitions based on current workflows and candidate operations. It combines structured state construction, cost-aware routing, and an LLM-based simulator, achieving a 14x acceleration in reinforcement learning agent training and 3-6x faster search-based inference, while outperforming the leading LLM baseline by 35.6% on transition prediction tasks. The framework is supported by an 8K-scale transition trajectory dataset and employs Reflective World Model Optimization for improved prediction accuracy.
items.judge: The introduction of a Data Science World Model relates to world models and anticipatory modeling, aligning with primary focus.

items.title: Xiaomi-Robotics-1: Scaling Vision-Language-Action Models with over 100K Hours of Real-World Trajectories
items.details: Xiaomi Robotics Team, Jun Guo, Piaopiao Jin, Jason Li et al. · 49 HF upvotes
items.summary: Xiaomi-Robotics-1 introduces a vision-language-action model trained on over 100,000 hours of real-world manipulation trajectories, achieving a new state-of-the-art success rate of 57.6% on the RoboCasa365 benchmark, up from 46.6%. The model employs a two-stage training approach, with a scalable auto-labeling pipeline that enhances action learning through natural language annotations, allowing for efficient adaptation to novel tasks with minimal fine-tuning. Extensive experiments demonstrate strong scaling behavior, indicating improved performance with increased data and model size.
items.judge: The paper presents a vision-language-action model, which is relevant to multimodal reasoning but not directly focused on generative media.

items.title: See like a Robot: Robot-Centric Pointmaps for Vision-Language-Action Models
items.details: Byungkun Lee, Dongyoon Hwang, Dongjin Kim, Hojoon Lee et al. · 2 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces robot-centric pointmaps, which represent 3D scene coordinates in the robot's frame, addressing the frame mismatch in vision-language-action (VLA) models. This approach allows for seamless integration into existing VLA architectures with minimal changes and significantly improves performance on the RoboCasa dataset, outperforming traditional camera-viewpoint and 3D-aware baselines. In real-robot experiments, the advantage of using pointmaps over RGB-only policies increases when the camera is repositioned, demonstrating enhanced generalization across diverse viewpoints.
items.judge: The paper discusses vision-language-action models, which relate to multimodal reasoning.

items.title: Agon: Competitive Cross-Model RL with Implicit Rival Grading of Reasoning
items.details: Vladislav Beliaev · 1 HF upvotes
items.summary: Agon introduces a novel approach to reinforcement learning by employing two competing models that implicitly grade each other's reasoning during training, rather than relying solely on final answers. This method significantly improves performance on challenging tasks, achieving a doubling of the pass@1 rate on the DeepMath benchmark with Qwen3, which is approximately eight times the improvement seen with an untrained Mixture-of-Agents model. The design allows for a two-stage inference process where one model drafts a solution and the other evaluates it, paving the way for future enhancements in collaborative reasoning within latent space.
items.judge: The paper introduces a competitive RL framework, relevant to reinforcement learning techniques.

items.title: RESOURCE2SKILL: Distilling Executable Agent Skills from Human-Created Multimodal Resources
items.details: Yijia Fan, Zonglin Di, Zimo Wen, Yifan Yang et al. · 87 HF upvotes
items.summary: RESOURCE2SKILL introduces a novel framework that distills executable skills for software agents from diverse multimodal resources, including tutorial videos and articles, organized into a hierarchical Skill Wiki. This approach enhances agent performance, achieving an average score improvement of +11.9 percentage points over no-skill agents and outperforming strong baselines in 26 of 28 evaluated domains. Key design choices include the integration of structured text, code, and visual examples to leverage complementary signals from various resource types, along with an online skill acquisition mechanism for dynamic learning.
items.judge: The paper distills multimodal resources into executable skills, relevant to multimodal applications.

items.title: Beyond Entropy: Correctness-Aware Advantage Shaping via Contrastive Policy Optimization
items.details: Weiwen Xu, Jia Liu, Hou Pong Chan, Long Li et al. · 7 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces Contrastive Policy Optimization (CPO), a novel approach for advantage shaping in reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR), which improves upon traditional entropy-based methods by using token-level contrastive disagreement to better distinguish useful uncertainty from confusion. Empirical results demonstrate that CPO significantly outperforms entropy-based RLVR methods across various benchmarks while effectively addressing the zero-advantage problem and enhancing generalization. The method also provides insights into balancing exploration and exploitation by leveraging correct and incorrect responses.
items.judge: The paper proposes a new method for advantage shaping in RL, which is relevant to generative media.

items.title: kvcache-ai/ktransformers

items.title: BadWAM: When World-Action Models Dream Right but Act Wrong
items.details: Qi Li, Xingyi Yang, Xinchao Wang · 46 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces BadWAM, a framework that identifies and evaluates World-Action Drift Attacks on World-Action Models (WAMs), demonstrating that the assumed robustness of WAMs is fragile. The authors present two types of adversarial attacks: an action-only attack that reduces task success rates from 96.5% to 43.1%, and an imagination-preserving attack that induces harmful action shifts while maintaining plausible future predictions. This work highlights critical vulnerabilities in WAMs, suggesting that moderate future-preserving regularization can mitigate some effects of these attacks.
items.judge: The paper presents a framework for world-action models, relevant to physical simulation and embodied control.

items.title: Hierarchical Denoising For Multi-Step Visual Reasoning
items.details: Zezhong Qian, Xiaowei Chi, Chak-Wing Mak, Tianze Zhou et al. · 3 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces HDR (Hierarchical Denoising for Visual Reasoning), a novel framework that enhances multi-step reasoning in video generation by organizing video latents into a tree-structured hierarchy, allowing for efficient coarse-to-fine reasoning. HDR achieves a significant improvement in success rates from 34.22% to 60.29% (76.2% relative gain) and maintains low-latency streaming at 0.70 seconds per latent, which is 54.2 times faster than bidirectional diffusion models. Additionally, it retains 82.9% of full-data performance with only 2% of the training data, demonstrating its efficiency and effectiveness in complex reasoning tasks.
items.judge: The paper addresses video models and reasoning, relevant to generative video.

items.title: Demystifying On-Policy Distillation: Roles, Pathologies, and Regulations
items.details: Rui Wang, Hongru Wang, Yi Chen, Boyang Xue et al. · 18 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper presents a systematic analysis of on-policy distillation (OPD) in LLM post-training, identifying its role as an exploration catalyst and revealing two critical pathologies: Student-Teacher Mismatch and Length Exploitation. The authors propose lightweight signal regulations, such as advantage clipping and log-scale compression, which effectively mitigate these issues, leading to improved exploration and performance across seven benchmarks, outperforming existing OPD variants and RLVR baselines. This work emphasizes the importance of guiding signal quality over teacher model scale in successful OPD implementations.
items.judge: This paper discusses on-policy distillation, relevant to generative media training techniques.

items.title: MeanFlowNFT: Bringing Forward-Process RL to Average-Velocity Generators
items.details: Yushi Huang, Xiangxin Zhou, Jun Zhang, Liefeng Bo et al. · 12 HF upvotes
items.summary: MeanFlowNFT introduces a novel reinforcement learning framework that optimizes instantaneous velocities for MeanFlow generators while maintaining their fast few-step sampling capabilities. By leveraging the DiffusionNFT objective, MeanFlowNFT achieves significant performance improvements, outperforming prior state-of-the-art RL-tuned generators on 6 out of 8 metrics in the SD3.5-M benchmark and achieving a VBench score of 84.33 with just 4 sampling steps, surpassing the 50-step LongCat-Video RL model. This approach effectively bridges the gap between average and instantaneous velocity predictions, ensuring robust reward optimization for efficient image and video generation.
items.judge: This work on MeanFlow generators and reinforcement learning aligns with generative media techniques.

items.title: BadWAM: When World-Action Models Dream Right but Act Wrong

It characterizes two types of adversarial attacks—action-only and imagination-preserving—that significantly disrupt task performance, with the action-only attack reducing success rates from 96.5% to 43.1%. The findings highlight vulnerabilities in WAMs, suggesting that moderate future-preserving regularization can mitigate future imagination drift while still allowing for effective attacks.
items.judge: It discusses world-action models for embodied control, relevant to physical simulation.

items.title: Beyond the Leaderboard: Design Lessons for Trustworthy Multimodal VQA
items.details: Sushant Gautam, Vajira Thambawita, Michael A. Riegler, Pål Halvorsen et al. · cs.CL cs.CV
items.summary: The paper presents a critical analysis of nine multimodal question answering systems in healthcare, emphasizing the need for reliable and interpretable AI that integrates visual and textual evidence. It finds that while parameter-efficient adaptations of pretrained models yield strong performance, they often fail to ensure faithful clinical reasoning. The authors advocate for methods that enforce structured reasoning and explicit grounding to improve reliability across diverse question types, highlighting the necessity for evaluations that extend beyond traditional metrics to include explainability and robust data governance.
items.judge: The paper discusses multimodal VQA in healthcare, which has relevance to multimodal generation.

items.title: Symbal: Detecting Systematic Misalignments in Model-Generated Captions
items.details: Maya Varma, Jean-Benoit Delbrouck, Sophie Ostmeier, Akshay Chaudhari et al. · cs.CV cs.AI
items.summary: The paper introduces Symbal, a novel method for detecting systematic misalignments in captions generated by multimodal large language models (MLLMs), achieving a 63.8% accuracy across 420 datasets—nearly a 4x improvement over existing baselines. Additionally, it presents SymbalBench, a benchmark comprising 1.7 million image-text pairs from natural and medical domains, which facilitates the evaluation of automated misalignment detection methods. This tool enables users to audit MLLM-generated captions effectively without needing access to the underlying models.

items.title: Video = World + Event Stream

items.summary: The paper introduces Wan-Streamer v0.3, a generative model that conceptualizes video as a combination of a persistent world context and an event stream, enabling real-time predictions of how the world evolves in response to dynamic inputs. items.summary: VideoChat3 introduces a fully open, efficient, and generalist video-centric MLLM that improves video understanding by leveraging an Inflated 3D Vision Transformer (I3D-ViT) and Adaptive Frame Resolution for Streaming Video Perception, achieving significant efficiency gains in spatiotemporal representation. It features a scalable video data synthesis pipeline that curates three diverse datasets, enhancing generalization across various video types. Experiments show that VideoChat3 outperforms previous open-source models with equal or larger parameter counts, achieving superior performance with only 4B parameters.

items.title: RxBrain: Embodied Cognition Foundation Model with Joint Language-Visual Reasoning and Imagination
items.details: Haotian Liang, Mingkang Chen, Yufei Huang, Yuchun Guo et al. · 20 HF upvotes
items.summary: RxBrain introduces a novel embodied cognition foundation model that integrates joint language-visual reasoning and imagination, improving the representation of embodied plans by combining abstract planning structures with visual state predictions. Utilizing a unified multimodal Mixture-of-Transformers architecture, RxBrain effectively supports language, image, and video understanding and generation, demonstrating enhanced embodied understanding and generation capabilities in experiments. The model also shows promising performance in continuous robot action generation without the need for large-scale action-data pretraining, marking a significant advancement in the field of embodied cognition.
items.judge: The paper discusses a foundation model that integrates language and visual reasoning, relevant to multimodal generation.

items.title: Smarter and Cheaper at Once: Byte-Exact KV-Cache Grafting Turns a Frozen Small Model into a Verified-Knowledge Flywheel
items.details: Sietse Schelpe · 6 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper presents a novel method called byte-exact KV-cache grafting, which enhances the capabilities of frozen small language models without modifying their weights, achieving a significant performance increase from 80.0% to 93.3% on the AIME 2025 benchmark. This method allows for the restoration of verified knowledge as a byte-exact key-value state, resulting in a dramatic reduction in inference costs—solving previously unsolvable problems with 6,574 times fewer tokens and approximately 8,700 times less energy. Additionally, it expands the usable context from 32,768 to 2,854,766 tokens without requiring extra memory, ensuring byte-identical performance across machines of the same architecture.
items.judge: Discusses inference acceleration techniques, relevant to generative media.

items.title: SEED: Self-Evolving On-Policy Distillation for Agentic Reinforcement Learning
items.details: Jinyang Wu, Shuo Yang, Zhengxi Lu, Fan Zhang et al. · 79 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces SEED (Self-Evolving On-Policy Distillation), a novel framework that enhances reinforcement learning for interactive agents by converting completed on-policy trajectories into reusable skills, thus bridging the supervision gap between episode-level outcomes and token-level policy learning. SEED improves decision-making and sample efficiency through a dual optimization process that integrates hindsight skills with outcome-based reinforcement learning, demonstrating robust generalization across text-based and vision-based tasks. The implementation is available at https://github.com/jinyangwu/SEED.
items.judge: Explores reinforcement learning techniques applicable to generative models.

items.title: Concept-Guided Spatial Regularization for World Models in Atari Pong

items.summary: The paper introduces Concept-Guided Spatial Regularization (CGSReg), an auxiliary pixel reconstruction loss aimed at enhancing the performance of visual world models in Atari Pong. By applying CGSReg, the authors demonstrate significant improvements in closed-loop rollouts and pixel-space zero-shot model-based reinforcement learning (MBRL) for models like DreamerV3, DIAMOND, and TWISTER, with DreamerV3's mean return improving from -20.9 to -5.5. The study highlights the critical role of accurately modeling task-specific concepts, revealing that existing models exhibit substantial failures when evaluated in isolation.

items.title: DriftWorld: Fast World Modeling through Drifting

This model demonstrates state-of-the-art decision-making performance on various robotic manipulation benchmarks while maintaining high accuracy, and it can also function as an offline simulator for evaluating real-world robot policies, achieving correlation scores with ground truth of up to 0.99.

items.title: SceneBind: Binding What and Where Across Vision, Audio and Language
items.details: Mingfei Chen, Zijun Cui, Ruoke Zhang, Hyeonggon Ryu et al. · cs.CV cs.AI cs.MM
items.summary: SceneBind introduces a novel omni-modal representation that integrates semantic and 3D spatial understanding across vision, audio, and language, addressing the limitations of existing models that focus primarily on instance-level semantics. By combining a global semantic embedding with object-centric semantic-spatial slots, SceneBind achieves state-of-the-art performance in scene and spatial retrieval, while also enabling effective zero-shot transfer to tasks like audio-visual localization. The framework is trained on a new real-world binaural audio-visual dataset with structured annotations, enhancing its capability for cross-modal scene retrieval and object grounding.

This approach achieves a 76.2% relative improvement in success rates (from 34.22 to 60.29) and maintains low-latency streaming at 0.70 seconds per latent, outperforming bidirectional diffusion by 54.2 times in inference speed while retaining 82.9% of full-data performance with only 2% of the training data. items.title: HoloGeo: Mitigating Landmark Bias in Geo-localization via Evidence-Driven Reasoning
items.details: Pengcheng Zhou, Xuanyu Liu, Yanchen Yin, Bobo Li et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: HoloGeo introduces an evidence-driven reasoning framework designed to mitigate landmark bias in geo-localization tasks, improving model reliability by incorporating multi-dimensional rewards that promote balanced attention across diverse visual cues. It establishes two new metrics, Bias Intensity (BI) and Bias Harmfulness (BH), and a benchmark dataset, LandmarkBias-3K, to evaluate the impact of landmark bias. HoloGeo demonstrates superior performance on LandmarkBias-3K compared to existing open-source Vision-Language Models, validating its effectiveness in enhancing geospatial reasoning.
items.judge: The paper addresses landmark bias in VLMs, relevant to image localization but not directly generative.

items.title: TikStance: A Multimodal and Hierarchical Dataset for Multi-target Stance Analysis in TikTok Political Conversations
items.details: Yazhi Zhang, Fuqiang Niu, Bowen Zhang · cs.CL
items.summary: The TikStance dataset introduces a multimodal and hierarchical framework for stance detection in political discussions on TikTok, comprising 161 videos and 13,876 comments related to key figures in the 2024 U.S. election. It features a robust annotation process with a Krippendorff's \(α\) of 0.743 for Trump, 0.723 for Biden, and 0.722 for Harris, enabling nuanced analysis of audiovisual content and conversational context. This dataset facilitates advancements in multimodal stance detection and computational social science by linking video metadata to a structured comment tree, highlighting target-dependent stance distributions and conversational depth.
items.judge: Presents a multimodal dataset for stance analysis in TikTok, relevant for video analysis.

items.title: AsySplat: Efficient Asymmetric 3D Gaussian Splatting for Long-Sequence Scene Modeling
items.details: Yingji Zhong, Dave Zhenyu Chen, Fuzhao Ou, Youyu Chen et al. · 4 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces AsySplat, an asymmetric architecture for 3D Gaussian Splatting that decouples geometry and appearance modeling, resulting in a nearly 800x speedup in long-sequence novel view synthesis (NVS) while maintaining competitive performance against optimization-based methods. By utilizing coarse-grained tokens for geometry and fine-grained tokens for appearance, the model achieves significant parameter efficiency and reduces computational redundancy, outperforming state-of-the-art generalizable models in zero-shot scenarios with fewer parameters and lower training/inference overhead.
items.judge: The paper proposes an efficient method for 3D Gaussian splatting, relevant to generative 3D modeling.

items.title: From Pixels to States: Rethinking Interactive World Models as Game Engines
items.details: Zhen Li, Zian Meng, Shuwei Shi, Mingliang Zhai et al. · 22 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper "From Pixels to States" introduces a framework for interactive world modeling that rethinks game engines by emphasizing a recurrent action-state-observation loop, which enhances the coherence of player interactions in generative video models. It categorizes existing approaches across four dimensions—player action control, game state dynamics, state-observation persistence, and real-time generation—while presenting a scalable data engine for the game "Black Myth: Wukong," which includes over 90 hours of gameplay data with aligned player actions and game states. This work aims to advance the development of interactive game worlds by providing a comprehensive analysis of current methodologies and a valuable dataset for state-aware modeling.
items.judge: The paper explores interactive world models using video generative models, aligning well with generative media.

items.title: MultiRef-Compass: Towards Comprehensive Evaluation of Multi-Reference-to-Audio-Video Generation
items.details: Xiaohan Zhang, Yuqing Wen, Junlin Chen, Yuqi Tang et al. · 29 HF upvotes
items.summary: MultiRef-Compass introduces a comprehensive benchmark for multi-reference-to-audio-video (MR2AV) generation, addressing the limitations of existing benchmarks that primarily focus on text-driven generation and single-reference scenarios. It features 350 curated samples and an evaluation protocol encompassing four dimensions—Basic Quality, Reference Consistency, Audio-Visual Consistency, and Instruction Following—utilizing 14 sub-metrics for a robust assessment framework. Experimental results indicate significant performance gaps in current MR2AV systems, highlighting the necessity for this benchmark to guide future research in the field.
items.judge: This work addresses multi-reference audio-video generation, contributing to evaluation benchmarks in generative media.

items.title: KeyFrame-Compass: Towards Comprehensive Evaluation of Keyframe-Conditioned Video Generation
items.details: Yuqi Tang, Tengfei Liu, Yizheng Lai, Yuran Wang et al. · 30 HF upvotes
items.summary: KeyFrame-Compass introduces a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating keyframe-conditioned video generation, featuring 386 curated samples across various domains and settings. It presents an automated evaluation framework that measures both keyframe execution and overall video quality through six metrics, revealing significant limitations in current models, particularly a trade-off between faithful keyframe reproduction and natural video synthesis, especially under denser keyframe constraints. The findings indicate that many open-source models struggle with interpreting storyboard-grid inputs as temporally ordered sequences.
items.judge: The paper presents a benchmark for keyframe-conditioned video generation, relevant to generative media evaluation.

items.title: Concurrent Image Understanding and Generation: Self-Correcting Coupled Markov Jump Processes
items.details: Minh-Quan Le, Armand Comas, Alexandros Lattas, Stylianos Moschoglou et al. · 21 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces Self-Correcting Coupled Markov Jump Processes (SC-CMJP), a novel framework that enhances joint multimodal generation by allowing one modality's transition rates to depend on the other modality's confidence scores, thereby enabling real-time cross-modal corrections. The proposed CO_2Jump sampler, which operates without training and in a single pass, demonstrates superior performance in image understanding, editing, and visual reasoning tasks, achieving best-in-class results across three newly created multimodal generation corpora. The performance improvement scales with the number of denoising steps, indicating effective cross-modal coupling throughout the generation process.
items.judge: The paper explores coupled image understanding and generation, relevant to generative image models.

items.title: SPEAR: A Simulator for Photorealistic Embodied AI Research
items.details: Mike Roberts, Renhan Wang, Rushikesh Zawar, Rachith Dey-Prakash et al. · 5 HF upvotes
items.summary: SPEAR introduces a novel Python library that enhances photorealistic embodied AI research by connecting to Unreal Engine applications through a modular plugin architecture, exposing over 14,000 unique functions—an order-of-magnitude increase in programmability. It achieves rendering of 1920x1080 images at 73 frames per second, significantly faster than existing UE plugins, while providing unique ground truth modalities. SPEAR's high-level programming model allows for complex task execution with deterministic data dependencies, facilitating diverse applications from controlling multiple agents to scene editing via natural language.
items.judge: The paper introduces a simulator for embodied AI research, relevant to generative 3D and world models.

items.title: UniVR: Thinking in Visual Space for Unified Visual Reasoning
items.details: Zhongwei Ren, Yunchao Wei, Yao Zhao, Weibo Gong et al. · 20 HF upvotes
items.summary: UniVR introduces a novel reinforcement learning paradigm, VR-GRPO, which simultaneously learns complex reasoning, physical dynamics, and long-term planning from raw visual data, achieving up to a 25% improvement on the newly constructed VR-X benchmark. This benchmark evaluates diverse capabilities in visual reasoning without relying on task-specific heuristics or image-text pairs, marking a significant advancement in unified visual reasoning. The entire framework, including code, data, and models, is open-sourced for further research.
items.judge: UniVR investigates visual reasoning and dynamics, relevant to world models and multimodal generation.

items.title: VIABench: A Comprehensive Video Benchmark Collected from Blind Individuals for Visual Impairment Assistance
items.details: Yunfeng Liu, Yuandong Yang, Jiarui Han, Zhenpeng Huang et al. · 6 HF upvotes
items.summary: VIABench introduces a novel video benchmark tailored for evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) in assisting visually impaired individuals, featuring three core tasks: Proactive Reminder, Visual Question Answering (VQA), and Vision-Guided Interaction. The benchmark highlights significant shortcomings in current MLLMs, particularly in the Proactive Reminder task, where models struggle with real-time anticipation and responsiveness. This work aims to catalyze advancements in MLLMs for practical applications in visual impairment assistance, with accompanying code and data available at the provided GitHub link.
items.judge: The paper introduces a video benchmark for visually impaired assistance, relevant to video generation.

items.title: From Pixels to States: Rethinking Interactive World Models as Game Engines
items.details: Zhen Li, Zian Meng, Shuwei Shi, Mingliang Zhai et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: The paper introduces a framework for interactive world modeling in video games, emphasizing the need for coherent responses to player actions through a structured action-state-observation loop. It categorizes existing approaches across four dimensions—player action control, game state dynamics, state-observation persistence, and real-time generation—while presenting a scalable data engine for the game "Black Myth: Wukong" that includes over 90 hours of gameplay data with aligned actions, states, and visual observations. This resource aims to enhance state-aware game world modeling and facilitate advancements in the field.
items.judge: This work rethinks interactive world models using video generative models.

items.title: M$^\text{4}$World: A Multi-view Multimodal Driving World Model for Interactive Object Manipulation and Minute-long Streaming
items.details: Ke Cheng, Hanqiao Ye, Lei Shi, Yahui Liu et al. · cs.CV cs.RO
items.summary: M$^\text{4}$World introduces a novel multi-view and multimodal generative driving world model that enhances object-level controllability and long-horizon stability in autonomous driving simulations. It achieves stable minute-long video streaming through a multi-stage training framework that allows for online causal generation in just four denoising steps, while also enabling fine-grained object manipulation via a flexible conditioning interface. Comprehensive evaluations demonstrate high generation quality and precise controllability, making it a significant advancement for scalable driving simulation.
items.judge: This paper presents a multimodal generative driving world model, relevant to generative video and world models.

items.title: Music-to-Dance Generation via Atomic Movements
items.details: Xinhao Cai, Yixuan Sun, Minghang Zheng, Qingchao Chen et al. · cs.CV cs.AI
items.summary: The paper introduces a structure-aware framework for music-driven dance generation that models choreography as a sequence of atomic movements, addressing the limitations of previous methods that produced structurally incoherent dances. By segmenting and clustering large-scale dance data into interpretable atomic movements and employing a two-stage generation process, the proposed method achieves significantly improved structural coherence, rhythmic alignment, and perceptual naturalness compared to existing approaches. This framework enhances interpretability and allows for controllable editing through explicit structural representation.
items.judge: The work focuses on generating dance movements from music, which involves generative video techniques.

items.title: TCAM-Diff: Triplane-Aware Cross-Attention Medical Diffusion Model
items.details: Zhenkai Zhang, Krista A. Ehinger, Tom Drummond · eess.IV cs.CV
items.summary: TCAM-Diff introduces a 3D medical image generation model that significantly reduces memory requirements for encoding and generating high-resolution 3D data by employing a decoder-only autoencoder to learn triplane representations. The model integrates a triplane-aware cross-attention diffusion mechanism, achieving superior reconstruction and generative quality compared to existing encoder-decoder methods, as demonstrated by improved MSE and SSIM metrics across three medical datasets of varying scales.
items.judge: This paper presents a novel 3D medical image generation model, aligning with generative image models.

items.title: Kaleido: Algorithm-Hardware Co-Design for Video Diffusion Transformers by Exploiting Latent Space Correlations
items.details: Wenxuan Miao, Haosong Liu, Weiming Hu, Zihan Liu et al. · cs.AR cs.AI
items.summary: The paper presents Kaleido, a novel algorithm-hardware co-design that accelerates video diffusion transformers (vDiTs) by leveraging channel-wise spatiotemporal correlations in latent space. This approach introduces a lightweight channelwise reuse algorithm that reduces computation while maintaining generative quality, achieving over 17 dB improvement. Evaluations demonstrate that Kaleido provides up to 5.9x speedup and 16.0x energy savings compared to existing state-of-the-art accelerators.
items.judge: The paper discusses video diffusion transformers and their computational challenges, relevant to generative video models.

items.title: VideoRAE: Taming Video Foundation Models for Generative Modeling via Representation Autoencoders
items.details: Zhihao Xie, Junfeng Wu, Xinting Hu, Junchao Huang et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: VideoRAE introduces a novel representation autoencoder that effectively transforms frozen video foundation model representations into compact, generation-friendly video latents, achieving state-of-the-art class-to-video generative frame video distances (gFVDs) of 40 and 93 on UCF-101 with autoregressive and Diffusion Transformer generators, respectively. The model converges approximately 5x faster than existing autoencoder baselines and supports both continuous and discrete latent representations through a multi-codebook quantization approach. This work demonstrates the potential of leveraging frozen VFM representations for enhanced video generation capabilities.
items.judge: The paper discusses generative video modeling using representation autoencoders.

items.title: TRACE: Turn-level Reward Assignment via Credit Estimation for Long-Horizon Agents
items.details: Leitian Tao, Baolin Peng, Wenlin Yao, Tao Ge et al. · cs.LG
items.summary: The paper introduces TRACE (Turn-level Reward Assignment via Credit Estimation), a novel dense credit-assignment method for reinforcement learning that significantly enhances long-horizon agent performance without requiring additional training stages. By transforming gold-answer log-probabilities into log-ratio state values, TRACE improves the tool-use ability of the Qwen3 models on the closed-web BrowseComp-Plus benchmark, achieving performance increases from 7.2 to 35.6 for Qwen3-4B and from 8.4 to 42.6 for Qwen3-30B-A3B. This approach also accelerates convergence during training and transfers learned behaviors to open-web benchmarks.
items.judge: The paper addresses reward assignment for agents, relevant to reinforcement learning techniques applicable in generative media.

items.title: Peak-End-Net: A Peak-End Rule Inspired Framework for Generalizable Video Aesthetic Assessment
items.details: Geng Li, Haiwen Li, Rui Chen, Jing Tang et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: The paper introduces Peak-End-Net, a novel framework for video aesthetic assessment (VAA) that leverages the peak-end rule from psychology to enhance aesthetic judgment accuracy. By incorporating a pretrained image aesthetic assessment head for frame-wise aesthetic priors and an aesthetic rhythm encoder for temporal progression, the method achieves state-of-the-art performance on existing VAA benchmarks, demonstrating improved robustness under distribution shifts with a lightweight architecture based on a frozen vision transformer. The code and models are publicly available at the provided GitHub repository.
items.judge: The paper addresses video aesthetic assessment, relevant to video generation evaluation.

items.title: GigaWorld-Policy-0.5: A Faster and Stronger WAM Empowered by AutoResearch
items.details: GigaWorld Team, Angen Ye, Angyuan Ma, Boyuan Wang et al. · 21 HF upvotes
items.summary: GigaWorld-Policy-0.5 introduces an enhanced action-centered World Action Model (WAM) that improves robot control efficiency by utilizing a Mixture-of-Transformers architecture, achieving 85 ms inference latency on an RTX 4090. This model employs a mixed Action-Conditioned World Modeling (AC-WM) strategy during pretraining to strengthen the relationship between visual dynamics and actions, while an agent-based AutoResearch pipeline optimizes training configurations, significantly reducing hyperparameter tuning time. The design choices enable real-time closed-loop deployment without the computational overhead of generating future videos at inference time.
items.judge: The paper addresses world action models for robot policy learning, which involves future visual observations and video generation.

items.title: Discrete Diffusion Models: A Unified Framework from Tokenization to Generation
items.details: Ye Yuan, Weien Li, Rui Song, Zeyu Li et al. · 5 HF upvotes
items.summary: This paper presents a unified framework for discrete denoising diffusion models (DDMs), highlighting the impact of tokenization, vocabulary topology, and structural alphabets on model performance. By framing existing DDM formulations as variations within a common design space, the authors identify key trade-offs in training objectives, inference methods, and evaluation protocols, paving the way for enhanced model optimization and future research directions. This work aims to improve the efficiency and effectiveness of DDMs compared to traditional autoregressive models.
items.judge: Introduces a unified framework for discrete diffusion models, relevant to generative image generation.

items.title: Boogu-Image-0.1: Boosting Open-Source Unified Multimodal Understanding and Generation
items.details: Guoxuan Chen, Chufeng Xiao, Haoran Yang, Siyue Xie et al. · 105 HF upvotes
items.summary: Boogu-Image-0.1 is an open-source unified multimodal model family that includes Base, Turbo, Edit, and Edit-Turbo variants, achieving competitive performance in text-to-image generation and instruction-based editing with only 208.62 million unique images. It demonstrates significant improvements in generation and editing capabilities through enhanced model understanding, data quality, and efficient training pipelines, with a theoretical training cost of approximately $400K. The model consistently matches or surpasses existing open-source benchmarks and approaches the performance of leading closed-source systems, with all resources available under Apache 2.0.
items.judge: The paper presents an open-source multimodal model for text-to-image generation.

items.title: MetaView: Monocular Novel View Synthesis with Scale-Aware Implicit Geometry Priors
items.details: Yufei Cai, Xuesong Niu, Hao Lu, Kun Gai et al. · 21 HF upvotes
items.summary: MetaView introduces a novel diffusion-based framework for monocular novel view synthesis that effectively combines implicit geometry modeling with essential explicit 3D cues, achieving improved geometry consistency and precise camera control during large viewpoint changes. This approach significantly outperforms existing methods in terms of generalization and spatial structure perception, as demonstrated through extensive experiments. The code is available at https://github.com/KlingAIResearch/MetaView.
items.judge: The paper discusses novel view synthesis with implicit geometry priors, relevant to generative image models.

items.title: MAGIC: Transition-Aware Generation of Navigable Multi-Scene Game Worlds with Large Language Models
items.details: Tsz Hei Fan, Choi Wing Fung, Yuxuan Wan, Shuqing Li et al. · 2 HF upvotes
items.summary: MAGIC introduces a novel four-stage pipeline for generating navigable multi-scene game worlds from a single natural-language prompt, addressing challenges in cross-scene consistency, in-scene navigability, and transition evaluation. It achieves 0.99 precision, 0.95 recall, and 0.96 F1 on a benchmark of 100 multi-scene cases, significantly outperforming existing LLM baselines and Holodeck in producing executable projects with improved navigability. The system includes a transition-focused evaluation agent that validates scene transitions, enhancing the fidelity of multi-scene generation. The implementation is available at https://github.com/sereneee1201/MAGIC/.
items.judge: The paper discusses generating navigable multi-scene game worlds, relevant to 3D generation and multimodal models.

items.title: Hallo4D: Multi-Modal Hallucination Mitigation for Consistent Spatio-Temporal Generation
items.details: Hongbo Wang, Huaibo Huang, Jie Cao, Jin Liu et al. · 11 HF upvotes
items.summary: Hallo4D introduces a novel framework for mitigating spatiotemporal hallucinations in 3D and 4D content generation, significantly improving geometric consistency by employing a generation-detection-correction paradigm that utilizes large multimodal language models (LMMs) for identifying and correcting inconsistencies. The framework enhances temporal consistency and optimization efficiency through techniques such as motion-aware keyframe sampling and LMM-guided initialization, outperforming existing methods in extensive experiments across various generation settings. This model-agnostic approach allows for effective corrections without the need for retraining or architectural changes.
items.judge: The work addresses 3D generation and spatio-temporal consistency, relevant to video generation.

items.title: SynthDocBench: Controlled Benchmark for Long-Context Visual Document Understanding
items.details: Abhigya Verma, Khyati Mahajan, Amit Kumar Saha, Shruthan Radhakrishna et al. · 63 HF upvotes
items.summary: SynthDocBench introduces a synthetic benchmark for long-context visual document understanding, systematically controlling factors such as document length, layout structure, modality composition, and question type. This benchmark reveals critical failure modes in seven state-of-the-art vision language models, including an 8.3 percentage point decline in performance as document length increases, highlighting potential overfitting to existing benchmarks. The design employs a combinatorial approach and generates documents using an LLM pipeline, ensuring greater length and structural diversity than prior benchmarks.
items.judge: The paper introduces a benchmark for visual document understanding, relevant to VLMs.

items.title: Self in Space: Benchmarking Self-Awareness and Spatial Cognition in UAV Embodied Intelligence
items.details: Zhishan Zou, Guoyan Sun, Zhiwei Wei, Jiancheng Pan et al. · 2 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces SIS-Bench, a new benchmark for evaluating embodied spatial intelligence in UAVs, focusing on both spatial cognition and self-awareness, which are often treated separately in existing models. The benchmark features 4,856 question-answer pairs across 13 tasks, revealing that current multimodal large language models (MLLMs) struggle with agent-centered processes, particularly showing a performance drop in self-awareness. The authors propose a motion-aware representation that integrates self-related dynamics, demonstrating significant improvements in perception and memory performance, which enhances both spatial cognition and self-awareness in UAV decision-making tasks.
items.judge: Explores the use of multimodal models in UAV systems, relevant to generative media.

items.title: Inkling: Our Open-Weights Model
items.details: 1133 HN pts · [discussion](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48924912)
items.summary: Inkling is an open-weights generative model designed to enhance the efficiency of training large-scale neural networks by leveraging a novel architecture that reduces computational overhead by 30% compared to traditional models. It incorporates a modular design that allows for easy integration with existing frameworks, facilitating rapid experimentation and deployment in generative tasks. The model is particularly focused on improving image synthesis quality while maintaining lower resource requirements.
items.judge: This entry discusses an open-weights model, relevant to generative media.

items.title: How to Train a Gen AI Kick Drum Model on Your Old Linux Desktop with 6GB VRAM
items.details: 4 HN pts · [discussion](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48935687)
items.summary: The article provides a detailed guide on training a generative AI model specifically for kick drum synthesis using diffusion techniques on a Linux desktop with limited resources (6GB VRAM). It outlines the model architecture, training process, and optimization strategies that enable effective training despite hardware constraints, making it accessible for researchers with similar setups. Key design choices include the use of a lightweight diffusion model and efficient data augmentation techniques to enhance performance.
items.judge: The article provides a practical guide for training a generative AI model, which is relevant to generative media.

items.title: Controllable Generation of Diverse Dermatological Imagery for Fair and Efficient Malignancy Classification
items.details: Héctor Carrión, Narges Norouzi · cs.CV
items.summary: The paper introduces cgDDI, a hybrid framework for generating diverse dermatological imagery that enhances malignancy classification accuracy, achieving 86.4% with synthetic-only training and 90.9% with real data fine-tuning. The framework synthesizes realistic healthy skin samples, maps rare lesions to various skin tones, and allows efficient generation with as few as 10 training samples, significantly expanding a dataset by over 400x. The authors provide an open-source release of 266k+ synthetic images, code, and models to facilitate further research in equitable dermatological diagnosis.
items.judge: The paper presents a framework for controllable generation of dermatological imagery, directly related to generative image models.

items.title: AVSCap: Orchestrating Audio-Visual Synergy for Omni-modal Video Captioning
items.details: Yanghai Wang, Jiahao Wang, Jiafu Tang, Yuanxing Zhang et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: AVSCap introduces a novel framework for omni-modal video captioning that emphasizes explicit cross-modal event binding, addressing limitations in existing multimodal models. The authors present AVSCap-7B, a 7 billion parameter model trained using a two-stage strategy that combines supervised fine-tuning with reinforcement learning, resulting in significant improvements in non-speech audio coverage and audio-visual synergy. Their evaluation on the newly created AVSCapBench benchmark demonstrates that AVSCap-7B outperforms other open-source models in generating comprehensive and contextually relevant captions.
items.judge: The framework for omni-modal video captioning directly relates to generative video models.

items.title: ExtraGS: Enhancing Endoscopic View Extrapolation via Diffusion-Guided 3D Gaussian Splatting
items.details: Cheng-Tai Hsieh, Jiwei Shan, Han Fang, Jianshu Hu et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: The paper introduces ExtraGS, a novel framework that enhances endoscopic view extrapolation by integrating diffusion-guided 3D Gaussian Splatting with an uncertainty-guided virtual camera sampling strategy. This approach actively explores blind spots to maximize information gain and employs a confidence-weighted fine-tuning method to minimize artifacts in generated views, resulting in state-of-the-art performance in endoscopic novel view synthesis across multiple public datasets. The method significantly improves the reliability of extrapolated views, addressing limitations of conventional endoscopic imaging techniques.
items.judge: The paper discusses enhancing 3D view extrapolation using generative techniques, relevant to generative image models.

items.title: FlowWAM: Optical Flow as a Unified Action Representation for World Action Models
items.details: Yixiang Chen, Peiyan Li, Yuan Xu, Qisen Ma et al. · cs.RO cs.CV
items.summary: FlowWAM introduces a dual-stream diffusion framework that utilizes optical flow as a unified action representation for World Action Models (WAMs), addressing the challenge of aligning action representation with pretrained video generators. This method enables FlowWAM to achieve a success rate of 92.94% on the RoboTwin manipulation task and an EWMScore of 63.71 on WorldArena, marking an 18.4% relative improvement in trajectory accuracy compared to existing baselines. The approach leverages large-scale action-unlabeled video datasets for pretraining, enhancing its applicability and performance in both action prediction and world modeling tasks.
items.judge: The paper explores world action models using video generators, relevant to generative video and world modeling.

items.title: The Seriality Gap in Video Diffusion Models
items.details: Jorge Diaz Chao, Konpat Preechakul, Yuxi Liu, Yutong Bai · cs.LG cs.CV
items.summary: The paper introduces the concept of the "seriality gap" in video diffusion models, demonstrating that performance degrades with increasing causal chain length in multi-ball dynamics, despite more denoising steps. The authors show that enhancing effective serial computation through methods like autoregressive generation significantly improves performance, highlighting a structural limitation in current video diffusion architectures for tasks requiring serial reasoning. This work underscores the need for architectural adjustments to better handle dependent-event structures in video prediction tasks.
items.judge: The paper discusses video diffusion models and their performance, relevant to generative video.

items.title: DermDepth: Toward Monocular Metric Scale 3D Reconstruction Models for Dermatology
items.details: Héctor Carrión, Narges Norouzi · cs.CV
items.summary: The paper introduces DermDepth, a novel monocular 3D reconstruction model specifically designed for dermatology, achieving metric scale measurements and detailed surface normal texture estimates from single-view images without additional hardware. The model reduces metric scale error from over 16x to under 1.1x when trained on the newly created D-Synth dataset, which provides pixel-perfect 3D information for dermoscopic images. The approach demonstrates strong generalization across diverse clinical scenarios, with all related code and datasets available at the provided GitHub repository.
items.judge: The paper discusses 3D reconstruction models for dermatology, relevant to generative 3D applications.

items.title: ViCo3D: Empowering LiDAR-based Collaborative 3D Object Detection with Vision Foundation Models
items.details: Haojie Ren, Songrui Luo, Lingfeng Wang, Yan Xia et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: ViCo3D introduces a novel framework for LiDAR-based collaborative 3D object detection that leverages vision foundation models (VFMs) to enhance feature-level collaboration among agents. By projecting point clouds onto a bird's-eye-view (BEV) plane and integrating DINOv2-extracted visual features with LiDAR geometric data through a multi-scale BEV fusion module, ViCo3D achieves state-of-the-art performance, demonstrating up to 1.8x greater collaborative gains compared to previous methods on the DAIR-V2X dataset. The code will be publicly available for further research.
items.judge: The paper discusses collaborative 3D object detection using vision foundation models, relevant to generative 3D applications.

items.title: UniMedSeg: Unified In-Context Learning for Multi-Paradigm 2D/3D Medical Image Segmentation
items.details: Yunzhou Li, Jiesi Hu, Yanwu Yang, Hanyang Peng et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: UniMedSeg introduces a Transformer-centric framework for unified medical image segmentation that integrates visual examples, geometric interactions, and language instructions across both 2D and 3D modalities, eliminating the need for prompt- or dimension-specific models. It employs Decoupled Split Attention to achieve linear attention complexity, enhancing computational efficiency while maintaining effective context interaction. The model demonstrates state-of-the-art performance on diverse segmentation tasks without requiring task-specific fine-tuning, validated on a large dataset from 27 public sources. The code and model weights are available at https://github.com/Lii1228/UniMedSeg.
items.judge: The paper discusses medical image segmentation, which relates to image generation and evaluation.

items.title: Search Beyond What Can Be Taught: Evolving the Knowledge Boundary in Agentic Visual Generation
items.details: Haozhe Wang, Weijia Feng, Jinpeng Yu, Che Liu et al. · 3 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces SearchGen-20K and SearchGen-Bench, comprising 20,839 prompts across twelve failure categories, revealing that current visual generators score only 21 to 28 out of 100 on the benchmark, indicating a significant 40-point performance gap. The authors propose a teach-then-search co-training framework that enables generators to discover and adapt to their evolving knowledge boundaries, resulting in consistent performance improvements for agentic visual generation. The accompanying datasets and co-training corpus are released to facilitate reproducible research in enhancing world-knowledge-grounded visual generation.
items.judge: The paper discusses evolving visual generation capabilities, which is closely related to generative image models.

items.title: Let RGB Be the Language of Vision
items.details: Timing Yang, Jinrui Yang, Xinlong Li, Yuhan Wang et al. · 2 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces RGB In and RGB Out (RINO), a unified framework for vision models that represents various forms of visual information, such as masks and depth maps, as RGB images. This approach allows a single model to handle diverse visual tasks through a common RGB-to-RGB editing paradigm, achieving competitive zero-shot performance on tasks like segmentation and pose-to-image generation without task-specific fine-tuning. The code is available at https://github.com/yangtiming/RINO.
items.judge: The work proposes a unified vision model framework, relevant to generative image tasks.

items.title: Read It Back: Pretrained MLLMs Are Zero-Shot Reward Models for Text-to-Image Generation
items.details: Runhui Huang, Qihui Zhang, Zhe Liu, Yu Gao et al. · 29 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces SpectraReward, a novel training-free reward function that repurposes pretrained MLLMs as reward models for image generation in reinforcement learning, achieving significant improvements in generation performance across various models and benchmarks. By utilizing the average image-conditioned prompt log-likelihood as the reward, it eliminates the need for preference labels and fine-tuning, while the Self-SpectraReward variant enables a self-improving framework that leverages the model's own understanding for reward assessment. Extensive experiments demonstrate that these methods outperform traditional MLLM-derived reward training approaches, highlighting the importance of reward-policy alignment over model size.
items.judge: The paper proposes a reward model for image generation, directly related to generative media.

items.title: Xiaomi-Robotics-U0: Unified Embodied Synthesis with World Foundation Model
items.details: Xinghang Li, Jun Guo, Qiwei Li, Long Qian et al. · 30 HF upvotes
items.summary: Xiaomi-Robotics-U0 introduces a 38-billion-parameter multimodal autoregressive model that unifies embodied synthesis by extending foundation image and video generation to include text-to-image generation, image editing, and embodied scene and video generation. This model achieves state-of-the-art performance, improving the out-of-distribution success rate of pi_0.5 from 36.9% to 63.2% on complex real-world manipulation tasks, while also ranking first on World Arena for embodied video generation. Notably, it maintains multi-view consistency and interaction dynamics, making it a significant advancement in the integration of foundation models with robotic applications.
items.judge: The paper presents a multimodal foundation model for image and video generation in embodied scenarios.

items.title: Latent-Identity Tuning in Text-to-Image Personalization Models

items.summary: The paper introduces a novel method for fine-grained identity tuning in text-to-image personalization models, allowing for precise facial edits without additional training. By modifying the latent representation of a specific identity within a frozen encoder's latent space, the approach enables diverse image generation that maintains consistent identity across edits. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of this technique in achieving localized, semantically coherent facial modifications.

This approach addresses the limitations of existing methods that struggle with generalization and data scarcity in varied skeletons, demonstrating significant performance improvements over baseline methods in extensive experiments. The project page is available at https://lhchen.top/Motion4Motion.

items.title: MonkeyOCRv2: A Visual-Text Foundation Model for Document AI
items.details: Yuliang Liu, Zhang Li, Ziyang Zhang, Shuo Zhang et al. · 2 HF upvotes
items.summary: MonkeyOCRv2 introduces a novel visual-text foundation model specifically designed for document AI, leveraging the largest document-image pretraining corpus, MonkeyDoc v2, with 113 million images across 17 languages. Its dual pretraining strategy enhances performance on five document analysis tasks, achieving a new open-source state-of-the-art in document parsing with a 0.7B model that outperforms a 3B model by 2.8% absolute while being 11 times smaller. Additionally, it demonstrates superior capabilities in document understanding compared to existing models like CLIP, DINO, and SAM across eight benchmarks.
items.judge: The paper presents a visual-text foundation model for document AI, relevant to multimodal applications.

items.title: Evidence-Backed Video Question Answering
items.details: Shijie Wang, Honglu Zhou, Ziyang Wang, Ran Xu et al. · 1 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces Evidence-Backed Video Question Answering (E-VQA), a novel task that enhances video question answering by requiring models to provide both semantic answers and precise spatio-temporal evidence, including tracked object segmentation masklets. The authors present ST-Evidence, a human-verified benchmark for pixel-level grounding, and demonstrate that fine-tuning grounded Video LLMs on their newly created ST-Evidence-Instruct dataset results in significant performance improvements over existing models, achieving gains of +27.2 t-mean and +13.8 J&F on a 7B model. The code and dataset are available at the provided GitHub link.
items.judge: The work on video question answering is relevant to generative video models.

items.title: StoryTeller: Training-Free Narrative Grounding for Long-Form Audio Description
items.details: Seung Hyun Hahm, Minh T. Dinh, SouYoung Jin · cs.CV cs.AI
items.summary: The paper introduces StoryTeller, a training-free framework for long-form audio description (AD) that enhances narrative coherence by maintaining a verified narrative memory across scenes, allowing for contextually rich descriptions without relying on subtitles or pre-existing scripts. StoryTeller significantly improves narrative coherence, factual grounding, and story comprehension compared to existing video-language models, as demonstrated by evaluations using the new StoryAD-QA benchmark and standard AD benchmarks. The method's ability to utilize public movie metadata and semantic filtering ensures that generated descriptions remain grounded in the visual content.

The authors present ST-Evidence, a human-verified benchmark for pixel-level grounding, and demonstrate that fine-tuning grounded Video LLMs on their newly created 160k-scale dataset leads to significant performance improvements over existing models, achieving gains of +27.2 t-mean and +13.8 J&F on a 7B model. items.title: Beyond the Single Camera: Agentic Multi-View Reasoning in Sports Video Understanding
items.details: Kerui Chen, Jinglu Wang, Xiaoyi Zhang, Yan Lu · cs.CV
items.summary: The paper introduces SportMV-Bench, a novel benchmark for evaluating multimodal large language models (MLLMs) on multi-view sports video understanding, comprising 787 video bundles and 2592 question-answer pairs across three categories. It highlights that existing MLLMs struggle with multi-view information due to limitations in fine-grained visual perception and view selection. The authors propose SportMV-Agent, an agentic framework that enhances performance through active view selection and evidence-grounded reasoning, achieving a 14.46% relative improvement over the best existing MLLM baseline.

items.title: LoRA-Based Cascaded Multimodal Fusion for Action Recognition in Medical Training Environments
items.details: Divya Mereddy, Jeevan Beedareddy · cs.CV cs.AI
items.summary: The paper introduces a cascaded Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA)-based multimodal fusion framework for action recognition in medical training environments, which allows for parameter-efficient integration of heterogeneous modalities without retraining prior components. This approach improves performance over individual modality models and achieves competitive results against existing dataset-specific baselines on the NurViD and Nurse Training datasets. The framework's design emphasizes scalable adaptation by sequentially integrating closely related modalities before adding others, enhancing its applicability across diverse datasets.
items.judge: The paper presents a multimodal fusion framework for action recognition in medical training.

items.title: From World Action Models to Embodied Brains: A Roadmap for Open-World Physical Intelligence
items.details: Yuanzhi Liang, Xufeng Zhan, Haibin Huang, Chi Zhang et al. · cs.RO cs.AI
items.summary: The paper proposes a co-evolution roadmap for achieving open-world physical intelligence through the development of World Action Models (WAMs), which connect interventions with predicted outcomes. It identifies three key gaps in current models—model roles and representations, objectives and standardization, and system composition—and suggests a modular stack for embodied agents that integrates multimodal context and enhances adaptability through shared contracts and closed-loop post-training. This approach aims to unify disparate models and datasets, facilitating more effective reasoning and action in physical environments.
items.judge: The paper discusses world models and their role in physical intelligence, relevant to generative media.

items.title: CtrlVTON: Controllable Virtual Try-On via Visual-Instance-Prompt Segmentation
items.details: Seungyong Lee, Hyun Jun Jang, Sangoh Kim, Sungjoon Park · 3 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces CtrlVTON, a controllable virtual try-on framework that enhances user control over garment styling, size, and placement by integrating Visual-Instance-Prompt Segmentation (VIP-SAM) for instance-level segmentation. CtrlVTON achieves state-of-the-art performance, generating images that adhere closely to user-defined layouts while maintaining high garment fidelity, outperforming leading proprietary systems. The framework recasts virtual try-on as an image editing problem, allowing for precise pixel-level manipulation of garment attributes.
items.judge: The paper presents a controllable virtual try-on system, relevant to generative image editing.

items.title: KronQ: LLM Quantization via Kronecker-Factored Hessian
items.details: Donghyun Lee, Yuhang Li, Ruokai Yin, Priyadarshini Panda · 17 HF upvotes
items.summary: KronQ introduces a novel post-training quantization (PTQ) framework for large language models (LLMs) that incorporates gradient covariance into the quantization process, significantly improving performance over existing methods like GPTQ. By leveraging the Kronecker-factored Hessian approximation, KronQ achieves a perplexity of 7.93 on LLaMA-3-70B with 2-bit weight-only quantization, while competing methods fail or produce poor results (>2000 perplexity). The framework enhances quantization by implementing bidirectional incoherence processing and a new sensitivity metric for mixed-precision allocation.
items.judge: The paper discusses quantization techniques for LLMs, which can be relevant for inference acceleration in generative media.

Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of this method in achieving diverse facial modifications while preserving identity integrity.

items.title: Cycle-World: Mitigating Error Accumulation in Long-term Video World Models via Reverse-Prediction Cycle Consistency
items.details: Zihan Su, Teng Hu, Jiangning Zhang, Ruiyan Wang et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: Cycle-World introduces a novel framework that mitigates error accumulation in long-term video generation by enforcing temporal reversibility through a cycle-consistency objective. This approach integrates a reverse-prediction model during training to embed causal constraints, and utilizes it as a runtime corrector during inference, significantly improving long-horizon temporal consistency and overall generation quality. Experimental results on the VBench benchmark indicate that Cycle-World achieves state-of-the-art performance in 60-second video synthesis, effectively addressing issues of generative drift and visual degradation.
items.judge: The paper proposes a framework for stable long-video generation, directly related to video world models.

items.title: ABot-3DWorld 0: A Universal World Model to Explore Any 3D Space
items.details: Mingchao Sun, Luyang Tang, Yu Liu, Xu Yan et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: ABot-3DWorld 0 introduces a novel multimodal 3D world model that integrates text, image, and video inputs to create high-fidelity, explorable 3D environments using a unified Spatial Generative Primitive (SGP). This framework enhances scene fidelity compared to existing open-source methods, outperforming Marble under rich multimodal inputs. The model facilitates both geometry-rigorous recovery from multi-view sets and generative completion from single images or sentences, enabling versatile 3D content creation anchored to geographic points of interest.
items.judge: This paper presents a universal multimodal 3D world model, directly relevant to generative media.

items.title: Feature-Space Guided Diffusion for Realistic Ultrasound Image Synthesis
items.details: Marina Domínguez, Nélida Mirabet-Herranz, Valery Naranjo · cs.CV
items.summary: The paper introduces Feature-Space Candidate Guidance (FSCG), a novel training-free sampling strategy that significantly enhances the realism of generated B-mode ultrasound images by addressing acquisition-dependent properties. FSCG achieves a 56% reduction in FID64 and a 57% reduction in FID192 compared to standard conditional diffusion methods, while also improving nearest-neighbor feature distance by 47%. This approach leverages local k-NN feature correction to align generated samples more closely with real ultrasound data without the need for retraining the underlying model. The implementation is available at https://github.com/marinadominguez/FSCG.
items.judge: The paper discusses a diffusion model for generating realistic ultrasound images, relevant to generative image synthesis.

items.title: Xiaomi-Robotics-U0: Unified Embodied Synthesis with World Foundation Model
items.details: Xinghang Li, Jun Guo, Qiwei Li, Long Qian et al. · cs.RO cs.AI
items.summary: Xiaomi-Robotics-U0 introduces a 38-billion-parameter multimodal autoregressive model that unifies embodied synthesis by extending foundation image and video generation to include text-to-image generation, image editing, and embodied scene and video generation. It achieves state-of-the-art performance, notably improving the out-of-distribution success rate of pi_0.5 from 36.9% to 63.2% on complex real-world manipulation tasks and surpassing GPT-Image-2.0 in human evaluations for embodied scene generation. The model preserves multi-view consistency and interaction dynamics, making it a significant advancement in the application of foundation models to embodied robotics. Code and checkpoints are available at https://robotics.xiaomi.com/xiaomi-robotics-u0.html.
items.judge: This work presents a multimodal foundation model for embodied scenarios, linking image and video generation.

items.title: MAGIC: Transition-Aware Generation of Navigable Multi-Scene Game Worlds with Large Language Models
items.details: Tsz Hei Fan, Choi Wing Fung, Yuxuan Wan, Shuqing Li et al. · cs.AI cs.GR
items.summary: MAGIC introduces a novel four-stage pipeline for generating navigable multi-scene game worlds from a single natural-language prompt, addressing key challenges of cross-scene consistency, in-scene navigability, and transition evaluation. The system achieves 0.99 precision, 0.95 recall, and 0.96 F1 on end-to-end transition identification across a benchmark of 100 multi-scene cases, significantly outperforming existing LLM baselines and Holodeck in terms of navigability and portal recovery. The implementation is available at https://github.com/sereneee1201/MAGIC/.
items.judge: The paper presents a method for generating navigable multi-scene game worlds using LLMs, relevant to 3D generation.
