items.title: Read It Back: Pretrained MLLMs Are Zero-Shot Reward Models for Text-to-Image Generation
items.details: Runhui Huang, Qihui Zhang, Zhe Liu, Yu Gao et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: The paper introduces SpectraReward, a novel training-free reward function that repurposes pretrained MLLMs as reward models for image generation in reinforcement learning, achieving significant performance improvements without the need for preference labels or fine-tuning. The method utilizes the average image-conditioned prompt log-likelihood as a reward signal, demonstrating effectiveness across various diffusion models and RL algorithms, with results indicating that it consistently outperforms traditional MLLM-derived reward training methods. Additionally, the Self-SpectraReward variant allows for a self-improving framework using the model's own understanding, achieving competitive results against larger external reward models.
items.judge: The paper discusses a reward model for image generation using MLLMs, linking it to generative image models.

items.title: PanoWorld: Real-World Panoramic Generation
items.details: Haoyuan Li, Dizhe Zhang, Yuemei Zhou, Xiangkai Zhang et al. · 7 HF upvotes
items.summary: PanoWorld introduces a novel approach to panoramic world modeling by leveraging rotation-equivariant omnidirectional representations, which simplifies camera trajectories into translations for improved long-range memory. The method employs Dense Panoramic Ray-Conditioning (DPRC) and Geometry-aware Memory Augmentation (GMA), and is validated on the newly constructed World360 dataset, outperforming existing techniques significantly in terms of physical consistency across varied spatial and illumination conditions. The authors provide their models, training code, and dataset as open-source resources.
items.judge: The paper addresses panoramic world models, which are directly related to generative video and 3D generation.

items.title: LATO.2: Factorized 3D Mesh Generation with Vertex and Topology Flow
items.details: Hang Long, Tianhao Zhao, Junkai Lin, Youjia Zhang et al. · 0 HF upvotes
items.summary: LATO.2 introduces a factorized flow matching framework for 3D mesh generation that separates vertex and connectivity flows, improving geometric fidelity and connectivity quality over existing methods. By utilizing dedicated VAEs and a shared coarse voxel scaffold, it enables part-wise generation for higher-resolution meshes and allows topology-adaptive editing, resulting in significant advancements in mesh generation capabilities. Experimental results demonstrate that LATO.2 outperforms state-of-the-art topology-aware mesh generators.
items.judge: The paper introduces a framework for factorized 3D mesh generation, relevant to generative 3D models.

items.title: From RGB Generation to Dense Field Readout: Pixel-Space Dense Prediction with Text-to-Image Models
items.details: Zanyi Wang, Xin Lin, Haodong Li, Dengyang Jiang et al. · 10 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces ReChannel, a novel approach that repurposes pretrained text-to-image models for dense prediction tasks by directly mapping tokens to pixel-space patches, rather than generating new RGB content. This method achieves state-of-the-art results on trimap-free matting, KITTI depth, and referring segmentation, while being 2.48 times faster and more accurate than existing edit-plus-latent-decode methods in a matched 4B setting. The design choice to retain the VAE encoder and adapt the DiT with task-specific LoRA allows for efficient integration of generative pretraining into dense prediction without the complexity of traditional output interfaces.
items.judge: The work discusses using text-to-image models for dense prediction, relevant to generative image tasks.

items.title: 4D Human-Scene Reconstruction from Low-Overlap Captures
items.details: Minhyuk Hwang, Sangmin Kim, Seunguk Do, Daneul Kim et al. · 16 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces StudioRecon, a novel pipeline for 4D human-scene reconstruction from sparse, low-overlap camera captures, effectively addressing artifacts in under-observed regions. By decoupling background and human elements, synthesizing novel views with a video diffusion model, and employing a recursive enhancement module, the method achieves state-of-the-art performance in novel view synthesis across four real-world datasets. Notably, it enables applications like novel trajectory rendering and human replacement, significantly improving output quality in scenarios with limited camera coverage.

items.title: Trust Region Policy Distillation

items.summary: The paper presents Trust Region Policy Distillation (TOP-D), a novel approach that transforms On-Policy Distillation (OPD) into a stable training paradigm by dynamically constructing a proximal teacher, effectively controlling gradient variance. TOP-D demonstrates significant improvements in training stability, sample efficiency, and final performance on mathematical reasoning tasks without incurring additional computational overhead, making it a compelling alternative to OPD. The authors provide a rigorous theoretical framework and empirical results to support their claims.
items.judge: The paper presents a distillation technique that could be applicable to generative media training.

items.title: Weak-to-Strong Generalization via Direct On-Policy Distillation
items.details: Shiyuan Feng, Huan-ang Gao, Haohan Chi, Hanlin Wu et al. · 3 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces Direct On-Policy Distillation (Direct-OPD), a method that enhances the efficiency of reinforcement learning by allowing a smaller model to generate rollouts and transfer its learned RL-induced policy shifts to a stronger target model, rather than directly distilling the weaker model's final policy. This approach significantly improves the performance of the Qwen3-1.7B model on the AIME 2024 benchmark, increasing its score from 48.3% to 58.3% in just 4 hours on 8 A100 GPUs, while also enabling the sequential composition of multiple policy shifts. Direct-OPD demonstrates that RL outcomes can be effectively reused across different model scales as implicit reward signals.
items.judge: The paper discusses reinforcement learning techniques applicable to model improvement, relevant to generative media.

items.title: ABot-N1: Toward a General Visual Language Navigation Foundation Model
items.details: Ruiyan Gong, Yingnan Guo, Junjun Hu, Jintao Kong et al. · 46 HF upvotes
items.summary: ABot-N1 introduces a novel Visual Language Navigation foundation model that decouples cognition from control using a slow-fast architecture, significantly improving urban-scale navigation tasks. It achieves a 35.0% increase in POI arrival rates (up to 77.3%) and maintains high success rates of 95.4% and 92.9% in complex indoor and outdoor environments, respectively. The model's design includes a slow vision-language reasoner for explicit reasoning and a fast action expert for real-time waypoint generation, enhancing interpretability and robustness across various navigation tasks.
items.judge: The paper presents a visual language navigation model, which is relevant to multimodal reasoning.

items.title: VaseMuseum: Digital Intelligent Museum for Ancient Greek Pottery
items.details: Jiazi Wang, Nonghai Zhang, Qiushi Xie, Zeyu Zhang et al. · 1 HF upvotes
items.summary: VaseMuseum introduces a novel multimodal agent framework designed for intelligent digital museums focused on ancient Greek pottery, addressing limitations in vision-language models (VLMs) related to evidence retrieval and response reliability. By integrating 3D-aware reasoning and a training-free GRPO-style selection mechanism, VaseMuseum enhances citation validity and reduces hallucinations in knowledge-intensive queries, achieving improved performance over existing search-enabled VLM baselines in a simulated digital museum environment. The framework emphasizes evidence-based responses and calibrated uncertainty, making it a significant advancement in the application of VLMs to cultural heritage contexts.
items.judge: The work involves vision-language models in a cultural heritage context, linking to multimodal applications.

items.title: LongE2V: Long-Horizon Event-based Video Reconstruction, Prediction, and Frame Interpolation with Video Diffusion Models

Extensive evaluations show that LongE2V outperforms state-of-the-art techniques across all tasks, demonstrating exceptional temporal coherence and zero-shot generalization.

items.title: ARDY: Autoregressive Diffusion with Hybrid Representation for Interactive Human Motion Generation

Evaluations on the HumanML3D benchmark and Bones Rigplay dataset demonstrate significant improvements in motion quality and constraint adherence, making it suitable for interactive applications in animation and robotics.

items.title: OPSD-V: On-Policy Self-Distillation for Post-Training Few-Step Autoregressive Video Generators
items.details: Hongyu Liu, Chun Wang, Feng Gao, Xuanhua He et al. · 8 HF upvotes
items.summary: OPSD-V introduces an on-policy self-distillation method for enhancing few-step autoregressive video diffusion models, addressing issues of error accumulation and motion dynamics during long video rollouts. By incorporating real long-video data as temporal context for training, OPSD-V provides dense trajectory-level supervision, resulting in significant improvements in visual quality and motion dynamics, as evidenced by user studies showing a 66.0% preference over baseline models. The method maintains the original inference path and cache mechanisms, ensuring compatibility with existing few-step AR video architectures like Self-Forcing and LongLive.

items.title: Enhancing In-context Panoramic Generation via Geometric-aware Pretraining

The framework improves panoramic image fidelity significantly, achieving superior performance on the panorama-specific FAED metric and demonstrating enhanced geometric consistency and global coherence through innovative modeling techniques.

items.title: OpenCoF: Learning to Reason Through Video Generation
items.details: Xinyan Chen, Ziyu Guo, Renrui Zhang, Dongzhi Jiang et al. · 13 HF upvotes

OpenCoF includes the OpenCoF-17K dataset, which spans 11 task families, and the Wan-CoF model, which demonstrates significant performance improvements over the Wan2.2-I2V-A14B baseline across four video reasoning benchmarks. The study emphasizes the importance of diverse temporal supervision and the integration of visual and textual reasoning tokens to improve spatial and temporal reasoning in video generation.

items.title: A Sovereign, Open-Source Foundation Model for German and English
