items.summary: The Soofi S 30B-A3B is a novel open-source Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) foundation model designed for German and English, activating only 3B of its 30B parameters per token, which significantly enhances throughput for long-context applications compared to dense models. Pretrained on approximately 27 trillion tokens with a focus on German, it achieves competitive performance against dense models ranging from 14 to 27B parameters and outperforms all European sovereign baselines, while also securing the highest evaluation scores among fully open models in both languages. The model will be released with comprehensive open-access terms, including weights, checkpoints, and detailed training documentation.
items.judge: The paper presents an open-source LLM, which is relevant but not directly tied to generative media.

items.title: Scalable Visual Pretraining for Language Intelligence
items.details: Yiming Zhang, Zhonghan Zhao, Wenwei Zhang, Haiteng Zhao et al. · 0 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces a novel approach to pretraining language models by incorporating visual representations directly from documents, rather than converting them to text, which has been the standard practice. This method, termed Visual Pretraining, demonstrates consistent performance improvements over traditional text-only pretraining across various model architectures and benchmarks, suggesting a more efficient pathway to enhancing language intelligence. The findings indicate that leveraging visual cues can significantly enrich the learning process, though specific numerical improvements are not detailed in the summary.
items.judge: The paper addresses visual representations in pretraining, relevant to multimodal models.

items.title: microsoft/TRELLIS.2
items.details: `python` · 8,708★ · 22 stars today
items.summary: The TRELLIS.2 repository introduces a novel approach for 3D generation using native and compact structured latents, significantly improving the efficiency of 3D model generation. By leveraging structured latents, the model reduces memory consumption and enhances the quality of generated outputs compared to previous methods. Notably, the design emphasizes compact representation, which facilitates faster inference times and lower resource requirements.
items.judge: This project focuses on structured latents for 3D generation, aligning with generative media.

items.title: Comfy-Org/ComfyUI
items.details: `python` · 120,491★ · 125 stars today
items.summary: ComfyUI is a modular graphical user interface for diffusion models, featuring a graph/nodes interface that enhances user interaction and customization. It allows for flexible integration and manipulation of various diffusion model components, improving usability for researchers and developers in generative image and video tasks. The design emphasizes modularity, enabling users to easily construct and modify workflows tailored to specific generative tasks.
items.judge: This is a powerful GUI for diffusion models, relevant to generative image tasks.

items.title: Flash-MSA: Accelerating Million-Token Training with Sparse Attention Kernels
items.details: 21 HN pts · [discussion](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48884618)
items.summary: Flash-MSA introduces a novel approach to accelerate million-token training in transformer models by utilizing sparse attention kernels, achieving a significant reduction in computational complexity. The method demonstrates up to a 4x speedup in training time while maintaining comparable performance to dense attention mechanisms. This advancement is particularly relevant for large-scale generative models, enabling more efficient training on extensive datasets.
items.judge: This paper discusses inference acceleration techniques that could be applicable to generative media.

items.title: LongE2V: Long-Horizon Event-based Video Reconstruction, Prediction, and Frame Interpolation with Video Diffusion Models
items.details: Cheng-De Fan, Chun-Wei Tuan Mu, Chen-Wei Chang, Chin-Yang Lin et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: The LongE2V framework introduces a novel method for event-based video reconstruction, prediction, and frame interpolation by leveraging pre-trained video diffusion models, achieving significant improvements in data efficiency and perceptual quality. Key innovations include Autoregressive Unrolling and Adaptive Context Switching to reduce temporal drift in long sequences, and Reencoding Alignment with Cross Residual Correction for enhanced bidirectional consistency. Extensive experiments show that LongE2V outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods across all tasks, demonstrating superior temporal coherence and zero-shot generalization capabilities.
items.judge: The proposed LongE2V addresses video reconstruction and prediction using generative models.

items.title: ARDY: Autoregressive Diffusion with Hybrid Representation for Interactive Human Motion Generation
items.details: Kaifeng Zhao, Mathis Petrovich, Haotian Zhang, Tingwu Wang et al. · cs.GR cs.CV cs.LG
items.summary: The paper introduces ARDY, a novel streaming generation framework for real-time 3D human motion synthesis that combines high-fidelity motion generation with controllability via online text prompts and kinematic constraints. ARDY utilizes a hybrid representation of explicit root features and latent body embeddings, along with a two-stage autoregressive transformer denoiser that supports variable history context, significantly improving the balance between trajectory control and generative efficiency. Evaluations on the HumanML3D benchmark and Bones Rigplay dataset demonstrate ARDY's superior motion quality and adherence to constraints, making it suitable for interactive applications in animation and robotics.
items.judge: The paper discusses generating realistic 3D human motions, which is directly related to generative video and interactive applications.

items.title: HumanForge: A Human-Centric Deepfake Video Benchmark with Multi-Agent Forgery Rationales
items.details: Wenbo Xu, Zhimin Chen, Xiaojie Liang, Hengrui Liu et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: The paper introduces HumanForge, a large-scale benchmark for human-centric video forgery that addresses gaps in existing datasets by incorporating human-object and human-human interactions. It employs a novel annotation pipeline, Gen2Anno, which utilizes six specialized agents to generate over 18,000 high-fidelity video segments with structured annotations, enhancing the ability to evaluate zero-shot generalization and fine-grained reasoning in forgery detection. The dataset and code will be publicly available, facilitating further research in digital content forensics.
items.judge: The paper introduces a benchmark for evaluating human-centric deepfake videos, relevant to generative video models.

items.title: OPSD-V: On-Policy Self-Distillation for Post-Training Few-Step Autoregressive Video Generators
items.details: Hongyu Liu, Chun Wang, Feng Gao, Xuanhua He et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: The paper introduces OPSD-V, an on-policy self-distillation method that enhances few-step autoregressive video diffusion models by reducing long-horizon degradation while maintaining the original inference path. By incorporating real long-video data as temporal context during training, OPSD-V provides dense trajectory-level supervision, leading to significant improvements in visual quality and motion dynamics, as evidenced by higher VBenchLong scores and a user preference rate of 66.0% in comparative studies. The method is applied to existing models like Self-Forcing and LongLive without altering their sampling or denoising processes.
items.judge: OPSD-V introduces a self-distillation method for autoregressive video generators.

items.title: Do Transformations Reveal the Truth? Generative Residual Learning for Generalized AI-Generated Image Detection
items.details: Kutub Uddin, Nusrat Tasnim, Awais Khan, Mohammad Umar Farooq et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: The paper introduces GenRes, a novel framework for generative residual learning that utilizes a neural tensor network to model fine-grained relational features between original and transformed samples, significantly improving AI-generated image detection. The enhanced version, GenRes++, incorporates a learnable attention mechanism to aggregate features from multiple transformations, enabling better generalization and detection of unseen generative methods. Experimental results show that GenRes++ outperforms existing detection methods across various benchmark datasets, indicating a substantial advancement in the robustness of AIGI detection.
items.judge: The paper proposes a framework for detecting AI-generated images, which is directly related to generative image models.

items.title: OpenCoF: Learning to Reason Through Video Generation
items.details: Xinyan Chen, Ziyu Guo, Renrui Zhang, Dongzhi Jiang et al. · cs.CV cs.AI
items.summary: The paper introduces OpenCoF, a novel framework for video generation that emphasizes Chain-of-Frame (CoF) reasoning, addressing the limitations of existing models trained on general video datasets. It includes the OpenCoF-17K dataset, covering 11 task families, and the Wan-CoF model, which demonstrates significant performance improvements over the Wan2.2-I2V-A14B baseline across four video reasoning benchmarks. The study highlights the importance of diverse temporal supervision and the integration of visual and textual reasoning tokens to enhance spatial and temporal reasoning capabilities.
items.judge: The paper discusses reasoning through video generation, linking to multimodal capabilities.

items.title: Enhancing In-context Panoramic Generation via Geometric-aware Pretraining
items.details: Haoran Feng, Ruiyang Zhang, Longyi Zhang, Dizhe Zhang et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: The paper introduces Canvas360, a two-stage framework for in-context panoramic generation that leverages geometry-aware pretraining and task-specific fine-tuning. It addresses the scarcity of high-quality training data by presenting the Canvas360Dataset, which consists of 1M paired panoramic samples, and enhances text-to-panorama generation through techniques such as parallel depth generation and similarity loss regularization. Experimental results indicate that Canvas360 significantly improves panoramic image fidelity, particularly on the FAED metric, and demonstrates superior task coverage and modeling flexibility compared to previous methods.
items.judge: Canvas360 focuses on panoramic generation, relevant to generative image tasks.

items.title: Native Video-Action Pretraining for Generalizable Robot Control
items.details: Qihang Zhang, Lin Li, Luyao Zhang, Shuai Yang et al. · cs.RO cs.CV
items.summary: The paper introduces LingBot-VA 2.0, a video-action foundation model specifically designed for robot control, which improves instruction following and action precision through a novel semantic visual-action tokenizer. It employs a causal pretraining paradigm to avoid catastrophic forgetting, utilizes a sparse MoE backbone for efficient high-frequency inference, and implements an enhanced asynchronous inference scheme for real-time closed-loop control. The model demonstrates robust few-shot generalization across complex manipulation tasks in real-world scenarios.
items.judge: The paper presents a video-action foundation model for robot control, relevant to generative video.

items.title: Vidu S1: A Real-Time Interactive Video Generation Model
items.details: Jintao Zhang, Kai Jiang, Jintao Chen, Xu Wang et al. · 107 HF upvotes
items.summary: Vidu S1 is a novel real-time interactive video generation model that allows users to control video content via voice instructions, achieving 540p video output at up to 42 FPS on standard consumer GPUs. It utilizes TurboDiffusion and TurboServe to ensure infinite-length video generation without blurring or visual distortion, outperforming existing models across all test metrics while meeting real-time inference requirements. The model also supports user-uploaded images and customizable voice tones for enhanced personalization.
items.judge: The paper presents a real-time interactive video generation model with voice control, directly relevant to generative video.

items.title: CineMobile: On-Device Image-to-Video Diffusion for Cinematic Camera Motion Generation
items.details: Xuyao Huang, Zelai Deng, Xu Wang, Xizhong Xiao et al. · 8 HF upvotes
items.summary: CineMobile introduces a novel approach for on-device image-to-video diffusion, achieving a 40x speedup in generation compared to the Wan 2.1 architecture while maintaining comparable visual quality. The model employs a distillation-guided pruning strategy, a 4-step generator optimized through diffusion distillation and reinforcement learning, and a hybrid post-training quantization method, resulting in a compact model footprint under 1 GB. CineMobile can generate 49-frame 480p videos with a per-step denoising latency of 0.6s on an NVIDIA H200 GPU and 20s on a MediaTek Dimensity 8400, showcasing its efficiency for mobile applications.
items.judge: The paper focuses on image-to-video generation with cinematic effects, directly relevant to generative video models.

items.title: Flash-BoN: Instant Drafts for Inference-Time Scaling in Diffusion Models
items.details: Ruchit Rawal, Reza Shirkavand, Sayak Paul, Yuxin Wen et al. · 2 HF upvotes
items.summary: Flash-BoN introduces a novel approach to inference-time scaling in diffusion models by optimizing the generation of draft candidates through timestep truncation, layer skipping, and activation proxies, leading to significant efficiency improvements. It consistently outperforms existing guided search techniques across three benchmarks and model scales, achieving up to an 8% increase in AUC under fixed wall-clock budgets. Additionally, Flash-BoN enhances the effectiveness of reflection-based prompt optimization by 16% AUC, demonstrating its ability to increase candidate diversity and accelerate reinforcement learning post-training convergence.
items.judge: This work on inference-time scaling for text-to-image generation is significant for generative image models.

items.title: SAM-MT: Real-Time Interactive Multi-Target Video Segmentation
items.details: Ruiqi Shen, Chang Liu, Henghui Ding · 0 HF upvotes
items.summary: SAM-MT introduces a novel interactive framework for real-time multi-target video segmentation, significantly improving processing speed by decoupling latency from the number of targets. Leveraging Segment Anything 2 (SAM2), it achieves over 36 FPS for 10 targets through the use of explicit queries, decoupled masked attention, and sparse memory, while maintaining robust segmentation performance. This approach addresses the limitations of traditional methods that suffer from increased latency as target counts rise.
items.judge: SAM-MT addresses real-time interactive multi-target video segmentation, which is significant for generative video applications.

items.title: RoboTALES: Learning Reasoning-Guided Robot Policies via Task-Aligned Simulated Futures
items.details: Hanan Gani, Tejal Kulkarni, Madhoolika Chodavarapu, Nicklas Hansen et al. · 3 HF upvotes
items.summary: RoboTALES introduces a novel framework for learning task-aligned simulated futures in robot policy training, addressing the limitations of pretrained video generative models in maintaining task intent and action-conditional outputs. The framework features a hierarchical LLM-based planner for decomposing complex tasks into subgoals and a VLM-based critic for evaluating imagined futures, resulting in improved temporal consistency and coherence in actions. Evaluations on manipulation tasks from RoboCasa and LIBERO10 demonstrate that RoboTALES outperforms existing methods, particularly in long-horizon scenarios. The code and models are available at https://github.com/hananshafi/RoboTALES.
items.judge: The paper discusses using pretrained video generative models for robot policy training, relevant to generative video and world models.

items.title: A Quantized Native Runtime for On-Device Semantic Audio Generation
items.details: Matteo Spanio, Antonio Rodà · 1 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces "aria," a dependency-free native runtime for on-device semantic audio generation that implements the complete text-to-music pipeline of Stable Audio 3 (SA3) on various hardware, including Raspberry Pi 5. The key technical contribution is the quantization study, demonstrating that running the model at eight-bit precision incurs no quality loss while significantly reducing memory usage, enabling the 1.2-billion-parameter model to operate on devices with as little as 8 GB of RAM. The runtime achieves generation speeds that match or exceed the official implementation, starting approximately seven times faster, and includes an activation steering feature for controlled output generation. The code is available at https://github.com/matteospanio/aria.
items.judge: The paper presents a runtime for audio generation, which is relevant to generative media.

items.title: Video-Oasis: Rethinking Evaluation of Video Understanding
items.details: Geuntaek Lim, Sungjune Park, Jaeyun Lee, Inwoong Lee et al. · 42 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces Video-Oasis, a diagnostic suite designed to systematically audit existing video understanding benchmarks, revealing that 55% of benchmark samples can be solved without visual input or temporal context. This audit highlights a significant capability gap in state-of-the-art models, which perform only marginally above random guessing on video-native challenges. The findings aim to inform the development of more rigorous evaluation criteria for future Video-LLMs, with the accompanying code available at the provided GitHub repository.
items.judge: The paper addresses evaluation criteria for video understanding, relevant to generative video models.

items.title: AUTOMATIC1111/stable-diffusion-webui
items.details: `python` · 164,163★ · 117 stars today
items.summary: The AUTOMATIC1111/stable-diffusion-webui repository provides a user-friendly web interface for Stable Diffusion, enabling interactive image generation with customizable parameters. Notable design choices include support for multiple models, real-time previews, and a modular architecture that allows for easy integration of additional features and extensions. This tool enhances accessibility for users and streamlines the image generation workflow.
items.judge: This web UI is directly related to generative image models, specifically Stable Diffusion.

items.title: LMCache/LMCache
items.details: `python` · 10,384★ · 101 stars today
items.summary: LMCache introduces a high-performance key-value caching layer designed to enhance the efficiency of large language models (LLMs) by reducing latency in token retrieval. It achieves significant speed improvements, reportedly up to 10x faster than traditional caching methods, by utilizing an optimized data structure and efficient memory management techniques. This repository provides a modular implementation that can be easily integrated into existing LLM frameworks, making it a valuable tool for researchers looking to optimize model inference times.
items.judge: This cache layer can accelerate LLM inference, which is relevant to generative media.

items.title: Infinite Worlds with Versatile Interactions
items.details: Zelin Gao, Qiuyu Wang, Jiapeng Zhu, Jingye Chen et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: The paper introduces LingBot-World 2.0, which enhances the previous version with four key upgrades: it achieves an unbounded interaction horizon while maintaining output quality, supports real-time 720p video streaming at 60 fps through a distilled variant, offers a wider range of interactive actions and text-driven events, and implements a dual-agent system for dynamic world modeling. Notably, the architecture allows for multi-user experiences and is designed for efficient deployment on a single GPU with a primary 14B model and a lightweight 1.3B variant.
items.judge: The paper presents a model for interactive environments, relevant to world models and video generation.

items.title: EmbodiedGen V2: An Agentic, Simulation-Ready 3D World Engine for Embodied AI
items.details: Xinjie Wang, Liu Liu, Taojun Ding, Andrew Choi et al. · cs.RO cs.CV
items.summary: EmbodiedGen V2 introduces a generative 3D world engine that automates the creation of simulation-ready environments for embodied AI, significantly enhancing the efficiency of closed-loop learning. The system achieves 96.5% human acceptance and 98.6% collision success rates, with 83.3% of generated task-driven worlds usable without manual modification. Notably, online reinforcement learning in these environments boosts simulation success from 9.7% to 79.8% and improves real robot task success from 21.7% to 75.0%, establishing a robust infrastructure for training and deploying embodied policies.
items.judge: EmbodiedGen V2 is a generative 3D world engine relevant to world modeling and generative media.

items.title: Scaling Mixture-of-Experts Video Pretraining for Embodied Intelligence
items.details: Shuailei Ma, Jiaqi Liao, Xinyang Wang, Jingjing Wang et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: The paper introduces LingBot-Video, a large-scale, open-source Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) video pretraining model designed for embodied intelligence, which enhances the trade-off between modeling capacity and inference efficiency compared to traditional dense architectures. It incorporates a unique data profiling engine that enriches standard internet videos with robot-centric footage and employs a multi-dimensional reward system to align model outputs with physical rationality and task completion, addressing the limitations of existing video generative models. Comprehensive evaluations demonstrate its improved performance and efficiency as a foundational model for video applications in robotics.
items.judge: The work presents a video pretraining paradigm tailored for embodied intelligence, relevant to generative video models.

items.title: Discovering Geometric Biases in 3D Face Reconstruction: A Curvature-Aware Spectral Framework for Fairness Evaluation
items.details: Veronika Shilova, Emmanuel Malherbe, Giovanni Palma, Panagiotis-Alexandros Bokaris et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: This paper introduces a curvature-aware spectral framework for evaluating 3D Morphable Models (3DMMs) in face reconstruction, addressing the inherent biases from training data. By utilizing the Laplace-Beltrami Operator to create high-resolution curvature error maps, the authors present a new error metric that correlates more closely with human perception than traditional Euclidean distance metrics. The study reveals systematic biases related to age, gender, and ethnicity, emphasizing the need for curvature-aware evaluation to enhance fairness and geometric accuracy in 3D face reconstruction.
items.judge: The paper addresses 3D face reconstruction, which is relevant to generative 3D modeling.

items.title: Face-trace: Open-Set Attribution and Progressive Discovery of Synthetic Face Generators
items.details: Alessia Infantino, Claudio Schiavella, Irene Amerini · cs.CV
items.summary: The paper introduces "Face-trace," a novel pipeline for open-set synthetic face source attribution that effectively identifies known generators and discovers unknown ones, addressing limitations of existing closed-set methods. It achieves a closed-set attribution accuracy of 96.73% and a balanced accuracy of 71.25% in open-set scenarios, with clustering metrics (ARI of 0.81, NMI of 0.90, and purity of 87.74%) indicating strong performance in grouping unknown generators. The method utilizes frozen I-JEPA embeddings for classification and combines them with Forensic Self-Descriptors for clustering, allowing for incremental updates as new rejected samples are processed over time.
items.judge: The paper addresses synthetic face attribution, relevant to generative image models.

items.title: Selective Timestep Weighting and Advantage-Based Replay for Sample-Efficient Diffusion RLHF
items.details: Eric Zhu, Abhinav Shrivastava, Soumik Mukhopadhyay · cs.LG cs.AI cs.CV
items.summary: The paper introduces two novel strategies—selective timestep weighting and advantage-based replay—to enhance the feedback efficiency of reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) applied to diffusion models. By reweighting denoising steps and prioritizing informative trajectories, the authors achieve up to a 6× improvement in sample efficiency compared to existing diffusion RLHF methods, while maintaining generalization to unseen prompts. This work addresses the critical bottleneck of feedback inefficiency in real-world applications of diffusion RLHF.
items.judge: This paper addresses RLHF techniques that could be applicable to generative models.

items.title: MedPMC: A Systematic Framework for Scaling High-Fidelity Medical Multimodal Data for Foundation Models
items.details: Hyunjae Kim, Dain Kim, Pan Xiao, Serina S. Applebaum et al. · cs.CV cs.LG
items.summary: The paper introduces MedPMC, an automated framework that curates high-fidelity medical multimodal data from 6.1 million PubMed Central articles, resulting in 11 million image-text pairs. MedPMC demonstrates significant improvements in various tasks, achieving an F1 score of 93.2 for initial screening and enhancing zero-shot AUC by 7.1 percentage points over existing biomedical CLIP models, while using less than half the data. The framework, along with its corpus and pretrained models, is publicly available, aiming to advance the development of medical multimodal foundation models.
items.judge: The introduction of a framework for scaling multimodal data is relevant to the development of foundation models.

items.title: Dual Latent Memory in Vision-Language-Action Models for Robotic Manipulation
items.details: Hongyu Qu, Jianzhe Gao, Xiaobin Hu, Shaohuan Yang et al. · cs.RO cs.CV
items.summary: The paper introduces LaMem-VLA, a novel framework for Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models that integrates historical experience into the latent embedding space, enhancing the model's ability to handle long-horizon, temporally dependent tasks. By employing a dual latent memory system with coordinated components for memory organization, retrieval, reconstruction, and integration, LaMem-VLA significantly improves action generation in robotic manipulation tasks, as evidenced by extensive experiments on SimplerEnv and LIBERO. This approach allows for a more fluid interleaving of multimodal reasoning and historical context, addressing limitations of existing memory-augmented VLA models.
items.judge: The paper discusses vision-language-action models, which are relevant to multimodal reasoning.

items.title: Imagined Rollouts are Kinematic, Not Dynamic: A Diagnosis of Long-Horizon World-Model Failure
items.details: Finn Rasmus Schäfer, Korbinian Moller, Yuan Gao, Christian Oefinger et al. · 1 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces a novel diagnostic called imagined Kinematic-Consistency Error (iKCE) to address long-horizon failures in world models, revealing that these models often operate on a kinematic rather than a dynamic basis. The authors demonstrate that, using a DreamerV3 checkpoint, the iKCE is approximately two orders of magnitude higher than that of real-physics rollouts, indicating a significant gap in the model's ability to accurately simulate dynamics over extended horizons. This reframing allows for a clearer understanding of the types of errors that compound in world models, particularly in scenarios crossing physical regime boundaries.
items.judge: Addresses long-horizon world models, relevant to generative video and simulation.

items.title: Cross-Space Distillation: Teaching One-Step Students with Modern Diffusion Teachers
items.details: Anh Nguyen, Ngan Nguyen, Duc Vu, Trung Dao et al. · 1 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces Cross-Space Distillation, a novel approach that allows knowledge transfer from high-capacity diffusion models (Teachers) to compact, deployment-friendly models (Students) that operate in different latent spaces. By employing a lightweight latent interface called the Bridge, which maps Student latents into Teacher space, the method significantly enhances the performance of models like SD 1.5, improving its HPSv3 score from 5.4 to 9.4 while maintaining one-step inference and low latency. This advancement facilitates the distillation of heterogeneous large Teachers into efficient, deployable models without altering the Student backbone.
items.judge: The work on one-step diffusion models and knowledge transfer is significant for generative media.

items.title: WildCity: A Real-World City-Scale Testbed for Rendering, Simulation, and Spatial Intelligence
items.details: Xiangyu Han, Mengyu Yang, Jiaqi Li, Bowen Chang et al. · 4 HF upvotes
items.summary: WildCity introduces a novel city-scale multimodal dataset designed to enhance AI's ability to build spatial representations comparable to human cognition. Collected from autonomous fleets, it features 18 trajectories averaging 83.7 kilometers, addressing challenges such as dynamic objects and lighting variations. The project also establishes an urban-tailored reconstruction baseline and a closed-loop simulator, aiming to advance city-scale rendering and spatial intelligence in AI systems.
items.judge: The introduction of a city-scale dataset for spatial intelligence has relevance to multimodal generation.

items.title: JD Oxygen AI Item Center (Oxygen AIIC) V1: An Industrial-Scale LLM/VLM-Centric Solution for Item Understanding, Management, and Applications
items.details: Oxygen AIIC, Chan Long, Chao Liu, Chaofan Chen et al. · 13 HF upvotes
items.summary: The JD Oxygen AI Item Center (Oxygen AIIC) introduces an industrial-scale LLM/VLM-centric platform designed to enhance item understanding and management for JD.com's extensive catalog of tens of billions of SKUs. Key innovations include a "Semantic Search then Discrimination" architecture that supports high-throughput knowledge production with 94.2% precision and 82.8% recall, and a self-evolving LLM/VLM system that processes hundreds of millions of item updates daily, resulting in an 80.4% search-traffic coverage and a 37% reduction in item-information quality issues. The platform's design emphasizes efficient human-AI collaboration and a unified data service hub, enabling significant operational improvements across various business scenarios.
items.judge: This paper discusses an industrial-scale VLM solution, which is relevant to multimodal applications.

items.title: HunyuanOCR-1.5: Making Lightweight OCR VLMs Faster and Better
items.details: Gengluo Li, Xingyu Wan, Shangpin Peng, Weinong Wang et al. · 4 HF upvotes
items.summary: HunyuanOCR-1.5 introduces a lightweight end-to-end OCR-specialized vision-language model that achieves a 6.37x speedup in Transformer inference and a 2.14x speedup under vLLM, making it the fastest among lightweight OCR VLMs. It enhances model capabilities through an innovative Agentic Data Flow system, improving performance in long-tail tasks such as ancient-script OCR and multi-image text-centric QA. The model weights and training code will be released to facilitate further research and practical applications.
items.judge: Involves a vision-language model, relevant to multimodal applications.

items.title: LLM-as-a-Tutor: Policy-Aware Prompt Adaptation for Non-Verifiable RL
items.details: Yujin Kim, Namgyu Ho, Sangmin Hwang, Joonkee Kim et al. · 20 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces "LLM-as-a-Tutor," a novel framework that enhances reinforcement learning for non-verifiable instruction following by allowing a single LLM to function both as a judge and a tutor. This approach dynamically adapts training prompts to align with the evolving capabilities of the policy, resulting in improved performance across three complex benchmarks, outperforming both policy-unaware baselines and previous policy-adaptive methods. The design's append-only mechanism ensures a continuous increase in prompt difficulty, providing a self-calibrating training signal without relying on external difficulty schedules.
items.judge: This paper explores RL techniques in the context of LLMs, which has secondary relevance to generative media.

items.title: Show HN: FableCut – A browser video editor AI agents can drive (zero deps)
items.details: 72 HN pts · [discussion](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48845422)
items.summary: FableCut is a zero-dependency browser-based video editor that allows AI agents to drive the editing process, enabling automated video creation and manipulation. The tool leverages a user-friendly interface and integrates AI capabilities to streamline video editing tasks, making it accessible for both developers and non-technical users. Notably, its design emphasizes ease of use and flexibility, allowing for rapid prototyping and deployment of AI-driven video editing workflows.
items.judge: The project involves a browser video editor that AI agents can utilize.

items.title: FADRA: Frequency-Aware Diffusion with Residual Adaptation for Video Face Restoration
items.details: Jin Jiang, Jia Wang, Panwen Hu, Weiran Zhao et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: The paper introduces FADRA, a frequency-aware diffusion framework for video face restoration that enhances spatial fidelity and temporal coherence by integrating lightweight LoRA adapters and a Repeated Residual Adaptation Head (RRAH). This approach allows for iterative refinement of facial details guided by low-quality observations, significantly improving the recovery of facial structures and temporal consistency compared to existing methods, as demonstrated by extensive experimental results. The novel Frequency-Aware Loss further ensures the preservation of perceptually important details across multiple spectral bands, addressing common issues of temporal jittering.
items.judge: The paper presents a novel approach for video face restoration using a frequency-aware diffusion framework.

items.title: CAIRN: Cross-Room 3D Scene Understanding with Topology-Aware Large Multimodal Models
items.details: He Liang, Chenyang Ma, Yiming Zhang, Sangyun Shin et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: CAIRN introduces a topology-aware 3D Large Language Model (3D-LLM) designed for multi-room scene understanding, significantly improving performance over existing models by effectively reasoning across interconnected rooms and diverse object categories. It enhances object tokens with room-local relational context through a graph neural network and employs a hierarchical attention mask with geometric bias, achieving superior results on the newly proposed CAIRN-MR benchmark across various tasks, while maintaining competitiveness on single-room benchmarks.
items.judge: CAIRN focuses on 3D scene understanding with multimodal models, relevant to generative applications.

items.title: ProxyPose: 6-DoF Pose Tracking via Video-to-Video Translation
items.details: Ruihang Zhang, Felix Taubner, Pooja Ravi, Kiriakos N. Kutulakos et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: ProxyPose introduces a novel approach to 6-DoF pose tracking by framing it as a video-to-video translation task, requiring only a monocular video and a single marked pixel. This method leverages a fine-tuned video diffusion model to generate a synthetic proxy video, enabling accurate pose estimation without the need for additional inputs like 3D models or depth maps. ProxyPose achieves state-of-the-art accuracy in pose tracking, effectively handling challenging materials and occlusions, and demonstrates versatility in applications such as face tracking and camera pose estimation in complex scenes.
items.judge: ProxyPose addresses 6-DoF pose tracking via video-to-video translation, linking to generative video models.

items.title: Training-Free Acceleration for Vision-Language-Action Models with Action Caching and Refinement
items.details: Ryuji Oi, Hikari Otsuka, Kosuke Matsushima, Yuki Ichikawa et al. · cs.RO cs.CV cs.LG
items.summary: The paper introduces ActionCache, a novel external caching mechanism for Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models that significantly reduces inference latency by reusing past intermediate actions. This approach achieves inference acceleration of up to 11.75× and 34.43× for the flow-based VLA models π_{0.5} and GR00T-N1.6, respectively, while maintaining high task success rates in both simulation and real-world environments. ActionCache employs compact multimodal keys for efficient retrieval of similar past actions, enhancing real-time deployment capabilities for robotic manipulations.
items.judge: The paper addresses computational challenges in vision-language-action models, which can impact inference efficiency.

items.title: Lift3D-VLA: Lifting VLA Models to 3D Geometry and Dynamics-Aware Manipulation
items.details: Jiaming Liu, Qingpo Wuwu, Nuowei Han, Hao Chen et al. · cs.RO cs.CV
items.summary: Lift3D-VLA introduces a unified Vision-Language-Action framework that enhances robotic manipulation by integrating explicit 3D point cloud reasoning and temporally coherent action generation. It improves mean success rates by 10.8% and 11.1% on MetaWorld and RLBench, respectively, compared to the best prior VLA methods, and outperforms the strongest real-world baseline by 4 percentage points. Key innovations include a 2D model-lifting strategy for better spatial alignment and a dual-objective self-supervised framework (GC-MAE) that captures both 3D structure and physical dynamics.
items.judge: The paper discusses lifting VLA models to incorporate 3D geometry, relevant to generative models.

items.title: Bridging Physical Reasoning and Task Generalization via Visual Action Outcome Reasoning Alignment
items.details: Han-Jun Ko, Jr-Jen Chen, Haobo Yuan, Hsin-Ying Lee et al. · cs.AI cs.CV
items.summary: The paper introduces VAORA (Visual Action Outcome Reasoning Alignment), a novel reward design that enhances vision-language models (VLMs) in interactive physical reasoning by addressing hallucinated chain-of-thought reasoning and misalignment between reasoning and actions. VAORA employs two complementary rewards—Visual Alignment Reward and Visual-Action Alignment Reward—resulting in improved generalization across unseen tasks and environments, as demonstrated in experiments on PHYRE and Virtual Tool. The approach also incorporates smooth, dense rewards based on success probabilities from a pre-trained expert agent to enhance training stability.
items.judge: The paper presents a reward design for VLMs, which is relevant to multimodal reasoning.

items.title: AirflowAttack: Thermal-Airflow Adversarial Perturbations against Infrared Remote-Sensing Vision-Language Models
items.details: Cong Su, Jiaju Han, Xuemeng Sun, Chengyin Hu et al. · cs.CV cs.AI
items.summary: The paper introduces AirflowAttack, the first adversarial attack specifically designed for infrared remote-sensing vision-language models (VLMs), utilizing thermal-airflow turbulence as a perturbation prior. This method achieves a mean zero-shot scene-classification attack success rate of 48.5% across five CLIP backbones, significantly outperforming existing IR-specific physical baselines (27.7-37.0%) and reducing scene-classification accuracy of state-of-the-art VLMs by up to 38.2%. The study highlights critical vulnerabilities in the IR VLM ecosystem and demonstrates that the airflow prior enhances physical plausibility without compromising attack effectiveness.
items.judge: The paper discusses adversarial attacks on vision-language models, which relates to inference in generative media.

items.title: Analysis-by-Proxy: Localization Signals in VLMs Operating as Condition Encoders
items.details: Yoav Baron, Sara Dorfman, Roni Paiss, Daniel Cohen-Or et al. · cs.CV cs.AI
items.summary: The paper introduces "Analysis-by-Proxy," a framework that reveals how localization signals in Vision-Language Models (VLMs) are inadequately utilized when these models serve as condition encoders in diffusion-based image editing. The authors demonstrate that existing editing pipelines fail to extract spatial knowledge effectively due to a mismatch in representation, as crucial localization information remains hidden in intermediate layers rather than being accessible during single-pass conditioning. This work highlights the need for improved conditioning architectures to better leverage the spatial understanding inherent in VLMs.
items.judge: This paper investigates VLMs in the context of image editing, which is relevant to generative media.

items.title: SceneFrom3D: Geometry-Conditioned Outdoor 3D Scene Generation via View Scheduling with Object-Level Control
items.details: Geonung Kim, Jeongeun Park, Nuri Ryu, Di Liu et al. · 1 HF upvotes
items.summary: SceneFrom3D introduces a novel framework for geometry-conditioned outdoor 3D scene generation that automates view scheduling, addressing a critical bottleneck in existing methods. By constructing a directed generation graph to optimize the selection and interpolation of views, it enhances controllability through object-level conditioning, allowing for precise appearance and geometry adherence. Experimental results indicate that SceneFrom3D achieves state-of-the-art performance in generating high-quality 3D scenes, significantly improving both the efficiency and quality of the generation process.
items.judge: The paper discusses 3D scene generation, which aligns with the primary focus on generative models.

items.title: CanvasAgent: Enabling Complex Image Creation and Editing via Visual Tool Orchestration
items.details: Hairui Zhu, Yiying Yang, Tengjin Weng, Ziyu Lu et al. · 8 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces CanvasAgent, a multimodal agent designed for complex image creation and editing by orchestrating various visual tools through multi-turn interactions. It leverages CanvasCraft, a new dataset comprising 140K annotated executable trajectories and 10K RL task specifications, to improve the agent's ability to learn and adapt its actions based on evolving visual states. Experimental results indicate that CanvasAgent significantly enhances both the quality of final images and the effectiveness of the tool orchestration process compared to existing models.
items.judge: Focuses on complex image creation and editing, directly relevant to generative image models.

items.title: PointDiT: Pixel-Space Diffusion for Monocular Geometry Estimation
items.details: Haofei Xu, Rundi Wu, Philipp Henzler, Nikolai Kalischek et al. · 7 HF upvotes
items.summary: PointDiT introduces a novel pixel-space Diffusion Transformer for monocular geometry estimation, eliminating the need for complex hybrid architectures and loss functions. By directly operating on raw 3D point map patches and conditioning on image tokens from a pre-trained DINOv3, it achieves superior performance compared to existing latent diffusion models, producing sharper geometric structures and demonstrating increased robustness in ambiguous regions, such as transparent objects. This approach simplifies the architecture while outperforming more complex alternatives.
items.judge: The paper presents a novel approach for 3D reconstruction using diffusion models, directly relevant to generative image tasks.

items.title: Image2Sim: Scaling Embodied Navigation via Generative Neural Simulator
items.details: Zihan Wang, Seungjun Lee, Yinghao Xu, Gim Hee Lee · 0 HF upvotes
items.summary: Image2Sim introduces a real-time neural simulation framework that constructs high-quality interactive environments from posed RGB-D image sequences, significantly improving the scalability and fidelity of embodied navigation training. By decoupling 3D spatial anchoring from photorealistic observation synthesis, it generates nearly 20,000 interactive scenes and over 10 million navigation training samples, leading to strong performance improvements on major benchmarks and effective zero-shot transfer to real-world scenarios. This approach addresses the limitations of both real-world datasets and synthetic simulators, providing a robust solution for training navigation models.
items.judge: Introduces a generative neural simulator for embodied navigation, relevant to 3D generation.

items.title: MentalThink: Shaping Thoughts in Mental SVG World
items.details: Kangheng Lin, Jisheng Yin, Dingming Li, En Yu et al. · 10 HF upvotes
items.summary: MentalThink introduces a novel visual-symbolic reasoning paradigm for Multimodal LLMs, utilizing a think-with-SVG pipeline that enables models to generate and interpret scalable vector graphics (SVG) for multi-turn reasoning. This approach, which combines Supervised Fine-Tuning and multi-turn Reinforcement Learning, significantly enhances spatial understanding and reasoning capabilities, achieving performance metrics of 55.1% on VSIBench and 76.0% on MindCube. The framework allows for dynamic perspective taking and visual reflection by externalizing spatial hypotheses in a structured visual format.
items.judge: The paper introduces a visual-symbolic reasoning paradigm that generates visual representations, relevant to generative image tasks.

items.title: Gemma 4 Technical Report
items.details: Gemma Team, Sherif El Abd, Vaibhav Aggarwal, Robin Algayres et al. · 22 HF upvotes
items.summary: Gemma 4 introduces a new generation of open-weight, multimodal language models with architectures ranging from 2.3B to 31B parameters, significantly enhancing compute efficiency and reasoning capabilities. Notably, the 12B model features a unified, encoder-free design that processes raw audio and image patches, while the integration of a reasoning trace mechanism improves inference speed and long-context handling. The model suite demonstrates superior performance across STEM, multimodal, and long-context benchmarks, competing effectively with larger state-of-the-art open models.
items.judge: The paper discusses a new generation of multimodal language models, which includes advancements relevant to generative media.

items.title: Attending to Multimodal Generation One Token at a Time
items.details: Varun Gupta, Vineet Gandhi, Makarand Tapaswi · 2 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces a novel analysis of attention dynamics in multimodal large language models (MLLMs) during autoregressive generation, revealing that attention to images peaks at tokens requiring visual information and that instruction tokens are revisited during task transitions. By implementing causal attention blocking interventions, the authors demonstrate that their proposed test-time intervention can significantly enhance multimodal task performance, improving model responses by effectively directing attention to the relevant modality at critical moments. This work provides insights into token-level dynamics and offers practical strategies for optimizing MLLM performance in multimodal contexts.
items.judge: The paper explores multimodal generation dynamics, relevant to the secondary focus on generative models.

items.title: Rank-Then-Act: Reward-Free Control from Frame-Order Progress
items.details: Yuriy Maksyuta, George Bredis, Ruslan Rakhimov, Daniil Gavrilov · 4 HF upvotes
items.summary: The Rank-Then-Act (RTA) framework introduces a novel approach for learning control policies from expert video demonstrations without relying on environment rewards. By employing a Vision-Language Model (VLM) as a progress-based ordinal scorer and utilizing a correlation-based reward function derived from Spearman rank correlation, RTA achieves stable transfer across tasks and environments, consistently matching or outperforming existing video-based reward learning methods on various control benchmarks. This method emphasizes the effectiveness of correlation-structured supervision over traditional reward design, enhancing scalability in policy learning.
items.judge: The paper discusses a VLM framework for learning control policies, relevant to generative models.

items.title: yt-dlp/yt-dlp
items.details: `python` · 176,377★ · 282 stars today
items.summary: yt-dlp is an enhanced fork of youtube-dl, designed for downloading audio and video from various platforms with improved performance and additional features. It supports a wider range of sites, offers better handling of live streams, and includes options for downloading subtitles and playlists. Notably, it has a more active development community, leading to faster updates and bug fixes compared to its predecessor.
items.judge: This tool is a feature-rich audio/video downloader, relevant to media generation.

items.title: Show HN: Follow London Trains in 3D
items.details: 45 HN pts · [discussion](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48786455)
items.summary: The project "Follow London Trains in 3D" provides a real-time 3D visualization of London train movements, enhancing user experience by allowing interactive tracking of train locations and routes. It utilizes WebGL for rendering and integrates live data feeds from Transport for London (TfL), offering a more immersive and informative way to monitor train schedules compared to traditional 2D maps. Notably, the design emphasizes user interactivity and responsiveness, making it suitable for both casual users and transport enthusiasts.
items.judge: This project involves 3D visualization of train data, relevant to 3D generation.

items.title: Vision as Unified Multimodal Generation
items.details: Xiaoyang Han, Jianhua Li, Kewang Deng, Zukai Chen et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: The paper introduces SenseNova-Vision, a unified multimodal model that formulates computer vision tasks as a generation problem, enabling the model to handle diverse tasks without task-specific architectures. It leverages a newly created SenseNova-Vision Corpus, which converts various computer vision annotations into instruction-response examples, allowing the model to perform tasks like detection, segmentation, and depth estimation with competitive performance compared to specialized systems. The approach demonstrates that a single model can effectively integrate multiple vision capabilities, suggesting a scalable method for enhancing general-purpose foundation models.
items.judge: The paper presents a unified multimodal model for various visual tasks, including image generation.

items.title: From RGB Generation to Dense Field Readout: Pixel-Space Dense Prediction with Text-to-Image Models
items.details: Zanyi Wang, Xin Lin, Haodong Li, Dengyang Jiang et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: The paper introduces ReChannel, a novel approach that repurposes pretrained text-to-image models for dense prediction tasks by utilizing a pixel-space output interface instead of generating new RGB content. This method achieves state-of-the-art results on trimap-free matting, KITTI depth, and referring segmentation, while being 2.48 times faster and more accurate than existing edit-plus-latent-decode methods in a matched 4B setting. By adapting the frozen DiT model with task-specific LoRA and employing a shared token-local linear head, ReChannel effectively leverages generative pretraining for dense prediction without the complexities of traditional RGB synthesis.
items.judge: The paper explores using text-to-image models for dense prediction tasks, leveraging generative capabilities.

items.title: Point as Skeleton: Accumulated Point Cloud Enhanced Autoregressive Generation for Closed-Loop Autonomous Driving Simulation
items.details: Songbur Wong, Xiaosong Jia, Junqi You, Bo Zhang et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: The paper introduces "Point as Skeleton," a generative sensor simulation framework that enhances autoregressive video generation for closed-loop autonomous driving by integrating point-cloud skeletons and a novel Reset-and-Roll mechanism. This approach improves the quality of generated driving videos by stabilizing error accumulation and maintaining visual fidelity during closed-loop simulations, as demonstrated through experiments on nuScenes and nuPlan datasets. The implementation is available as an open-source repository at https://github.com/krauwu/point-as-skeleton.
items.judge: The paper presents a generative framework for driving video generation, aligning with generative video models.

items.title: Prompt-Adapter Context Routing for Parameter-Efficient Multi-Shot Long Video Extrapolation
items.details: Anna Córdoba, Adam Puente Tercero, Nerea Angulo Hijo, Mar Linares Tercero et al. · cs.CV cs.AI
items.summary: The paper introduces PACR-Video, a parameter-efficient framework for multi-shot long video extrapolation that enhances coherence and consistency without requiring full generator fine-tuning. By utilizing a frozen text-to-video diffusion transformer augmented with low-rank temporal adapters and a recursive prompt bank, PACR-Video significantly improves distributional quality and semantic alignment across six benchmarks, outperforming existing methods in identity consistency and temporal smoothness. The proposed Shot-Local/Story-Global tuning objective effectively balances visual consistency and narrative progression, demonstrating the capability of compact prompt routing for stable long video generation.
items.judge: Presents a framework for long video extrapolation, directly related to video generation.

items.title: PIPBench: A Profile-Inclusive Framework for Personalized Image Generation Evaluation
items.details: Yuhang Wu, Shuxiang Zhang, Wee Hian Ching, Chi Zhang et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: PIPBench introduces the first profile-inclusive benchmark for evaluating personalized image generation, addressing the gap in aligning outputs with individual aesthetic preferences based on historical user images and prompts. The framework employs a novel data construction pipeline that integrates psychological and demographic profiling for both real-user data collection and scalable agent-based generation. Initial evaluations using PIPBench highlight significant limitations in current methods, paving the way for new challenges and advancements in personalized text-to-image synthesis.
items.judge: This paper introduces a benchmark for personalized image generation, aligning with generative image models.

items.title: A Definition and Roadmap for World Models
items.details: Xinyuan Chen, Haoyu Guo, Shi Guo, Bingqi Jiang et al. · cs.AI
items.summary: The paper presents a comprehensive definition of world models, addressing the lack of consensus in the AI community regarding their structure, predictive capabilities, and construction methods. It outlines key technical aspects and proposes a staged roadmap for developing effective world models, aiming to unify approaches across various applications, including model-based reinforcement learning and video generation. This work serves as a foundational reference for researchers seeking clarity and direction in the rapidly evolving field of world models.
items.judge: Provides a comprehensive overview of world models, relevant to generative video and simulation.

items.title: Bridging Diffusion Pruning and Step Distillation with Teacher-Aligned Repair
items.details: Jincheng Ying, Li Wenlin, Minghui Xu, Yinhao Xiao · cs.CV
items.summary: The paper introduces a novel approach that bridges diffusion pruning and step distillation through a teacher-aligned repair stage, which enhances the efficiency of diffusion models. By implementing this method, the authors achieve a 20% parameter reduction in the EDM2-XS model, decreasing the inference cost from 63 to 1 network evaluation while improving the FID from 3.53 to 3.12. Additionally, a 30% pruning results in 88.029M parameters and an FID of 4.26, demonstrating significant performance retention despite increased compression.
items.judge: Addresses diffusion models and their optimization, directly relevant to generative image media.

items.title: ELSA3D: Elastic Semantic Anchoring for Unified 3D Understanding and Generation
items.details: Tianjiao Yu, Xinzhuo Li, Yifan Shen, Onkar Susladkar et al. · cs.CV cs.AI cs.LG
items.summary: ELSA3D introduces elastic semantic anchoring for unified 3D understanding and generation, enhancing text-3D interaction by structuring language and geometric reasoning along matched abstraction scales. The model employs a scale-aware octree tokenizer and Anchor Tokens to selectively route semantic cues to relevant 3D scales, achieving state-of-the-art performance in image-to-3D generation, text-to-3D generation, and 3D captioning while reducing FLOPs and inference latency by approximately 50% compared to its non-elastic counterpart.
items.judge: The paper discusses a unified 3D model for generating and reasoning about 3D assets.

items.title: AlayaWorld: Long-Horizon and Playable Video World Generation
items.details: AlayaWorld Team, Kaipeng Zhang, Chuanhao Li, Yifan Zhan et al. · 36 HF upvotes
items.summary: AlayaWorld introduces a novel open-source framework for generating interactive video game worlds through autoregressive synthesis, significantly reducing the labor and cost associated with traditional game development. By leveraging gameplay recordings and real-world videos, it allows for real-time user interactions such as combat and spell casting, while providing a modular architecture that integrates data preparation, model training, and deployment. The framework also includes reproducible pipelines and comprehensive documentation, facilitating further research and application in generative world models.
items.judge: This paper discusses a novel approach to playable video world generation, aligning with the primary focus on world models.

items.title: CGGS: Consistency-Augmented Geometric Gaussian Splatting for Ego-centric 3D Scene Generation
items.details: Zhenyu Sun, Xiaohan Zhang, Qi Liu, Huan Wang · 1 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces CGGS, a text-to-3D framework that significantly improves ego-centric 3D scene generation by addressing viewpoint consistency and geometric distortions. It employs a Multi-View Latent Diffusion Model fine-tuned with a consistency-augmented loss, alongside a novel Geometric Refiner utilizing an entropy-based Mutual Information Depth Loss (MID) for enhanced visual quality and geometric accuracy. Experimental results indicate that CGGS outperforms existing methods in generating coherent and semantically aligned 3D scenes.
items.judge: This work proposes a text-to-3D framework for ego-centric scene generation, aligning with generative 3D models.

items.title: Flex-Forcing: Towards a Unified Autoregressive and Bidirectional Video Diffusion Model
items.details: Xinyin Ma, Julius Berner, Chao Liu, Arash Vahdat et al. · 0 HF upvotes
items.summary: Flex-Forcing introduces a novel unified training and inference framework for video diffusion models that allows for both bidirectional and autoregressive generation, significantly improving video quality and long-video stability. The model employs a flexible chunking mechanism that adapts to device budgets and enables efficient autoregressive synthesis within chunks while maintaining global coherence through bidirectional inference. Experiments show that Flex-Forcing outperforms existing methods with a rigid inference schedule, achieving faster inference times and superior video generation quality across multiple benchmarks.
items.judge: The paper presents a unified framework for video generation, aligning with primary focus areas.

items.title: SynCity 3000: Bootstrapping Scene-Scale 3D Diffusion
items.details: Paul Engstler, Iro Laina, Christian Rupprecht, Andrea Vedaldi · 2 HF upvotes
items.summary: SynCity 3000 introduces a novel framework for generating globally coherent 3D scenes with fine-grained layout control by adapting a convolutional image-to-3D generator. This approach leverages a new synthetic data engine to fine-tune the model on scene-like data, enabling the generation of complex 3D scenes from dimetric images based on user prompts. The framework significantly improves the coherence and detail of generated scenes compared to previous methods, addressing the limitations posed by the scarcity of 3D training data.
items.judge: The paper presents a framework for generating coherent 3D scenes, directly aligning with generative media.

items.title: Parallelized Autoregressive Decoding for Omni-Modal Dense Video Captioning
items.details: Wenzheng Zeng, Siyi Jiao, Chen Gao, Hwee Tou Ng et al. · 10 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces a parallelized autoregressive framework for dense video captioning that significantly enhances generation efficiency and temporally grounded captioning performance. By restructuring the causal dependency graph to allow lossless parallel decoding of tokens with weak cross-event dependencies, the method improves inference speed while maintaining local semantic coherence. Experimental results show clear advantages over traditional token-by-token approaches, making it a promising advancement in omni-modal event grounding and captioning. The implementation is available at https://github.com/showlab/PadCaptioner.
items.judge: This work addresses dense video captioning using autoregressive models, relevant to video generation.

items.title: RynnWorld-Teleop: An Action-Conditioned World Model for Digital Teleoperation
items.details: Haoyu Zhao, Xingyue Zhao, Hangyu Li, Biao Gong et al. · 2 HF upvotes
items.summary: RynnWorld-Teleop introduces a novel digital teleoperation framework that utilizes a generative world model to synthesize high-fidelity egocentric videos from a single reference image, enabling the collection of diverse trajectory data without physical constraints. This system achieves real-time interactive generation at over 40 FPS on a single H100 GPU and demonstrates effective zero-shot Sim2Real transfer for bimanual tasks, significantly enhancing the scalability and efficiency of robotic learning by allowing policies trained on synthetic data to improve real-world performance.
items.judge: This work introduces a generative world model for digital teleoperation, relevant to the topic of world models.

items.title: RynnWorld-4D: 4D Embodied World Models for Robotic Manipulation
items.details: Haoyu Zhao, Xingyue Zhao, Siteng Huang, Xin Li et al. · 2 HF upvotes
items.summary: RynnWorld-4D introduces a novel generative model for robotic manipulation that leverages synchronized RGB, depth, and optical flow (RGB-DF) to create a unified representation of 4D dynamics from a single RGB-D image and language instruction. This model employs a tri-branch architecture with cross-modal attention and frame-wise 3D RoPE, achieving state-of-the-art performance in real-world dexterous bimanual tasks by producing temporally and spatially coherent predictions. The accompanying Rynn4DDataset 1.0 provides over 254.4 million frames of high-quality training data, enhancing the model's ability to align visual appearance with geometric structure and motion.
items.judge: The paper focuses on 4D embodied world models for robotic manipulation, fitting within the primary focus on world models.

items.title: ACID: Action Consistency via Inverse Dynamics for Planning with World Models
items.details: Gawon Seo, Dongwon Kim, Suha Kwak · 11 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces ACID, a decision-time planning framework that enhances action-conditioned world models by incorporating cycle action consistency, ensuring that actions inferred from predicted transitions align with the conditioned actions. This approach leads to improved planning performance across four world models and six diverse tasks, achieving comparable accuracy to baseline methods while significantly reducing planning computation requirements.
items.judge: This paper proposes a planning framework using world models, relevant to the primary focus on world models.

items.title: Augani/openreel-video
items.details: `typescript` · 3,847★ · 85 stars today
items.summary: OpenReel Video is an open-source, browser-based video editing tool that serves as an alternative to CapCut, emphasizing a no-installation, no-cloud-upload, and watermark-free experience. Its notable design choice is the complete reliance on browser capabilities, allowing for seamless editing without local software requirements. This tool could enhance workflows for ML researchers working with video data by providing a lightweight and accessible editing solution.
items.judge: This item is a professional video editor, relevant to video generation and editing.

items.title: pascalorg/editor
items.details: `typescript` · 17,298★ · 13 stars today
items.summary: The repository "pascalorg/editor" provides a platform for creating and sharing 3D architectural projects, focusing on user-friendly design and collaborative features. Notable design choices include a modular architecture that allows for easy integration of various 3D modeling tools and a web-based interface for real-time collaboration among users. This tool aims to streamline the architectural design process and enhance accessibility for users without extensive technical backgrounds.
items.judge: This item involves creating 3D architectural projects, which is relevant to 3D generation.

items.title: Generated Contents Enrichment
items.details: Mahdi Naseri, Jiayan Qiu, Zhou Wang · 1 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces Generated Contents Enrichment (GCE), a novel conditional image-generation approach that enhances sparse scene descriptions by explicitly modeling scene representations through enriched scene graphs. This method employs a jointly trained adversarial framework utilizing graph convolutional networks to predict additional objects and their interrelations, resulting in generated images that are visually plausible, structurally coherent, and semantically richer than traditional methods. Evaluations on the Visual Genome dataset demonstrate significant improvements in image quality and content richness compared to conventional image generation techniques.
items.judge: The study on Generated Contents Enrichment directly relates to conditional image generation and editing.

items.title: AnyBokeh: Physics-Guided Any-to-Any Bokeh Editing with Optical Fingerprint Transfer
items.details: Xinyu Hou, Xiaoming Li, Zongsheng Yue, Chen Change Loy · 2 HF upvotes
items.summary: AnyBokeh introduces a physics-guided framework for any-to-any bokeh editing that significantly improves upon existing methods by estimating source blur characteristics directly from a single image, rather than relying on all-in-focus reconstruction. This approach utilizes a signed circle-of-confusion map and disparity map to create a source-specific optical fingerprint, enabling spatially adaptive deblurring and defocus rendering without the need for test-time calibration. The method demonstrates superior fidelity and control in bokeh editing tasks, supported by a high-fidelity synthetic dataset, with code available at https://github.com/itsmag11/AnyBokeh.
items.judge: The paper addresses bokeh editing in photography, which is a form of image editing and generation.

items.title: From SRA to Self-Flow: Data Augmentation or Self-Supervision?
items.details: Dengyang Jiang, Mengmeng Wang, Harry Yang, Jingdong Wang · 11 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces Attention Separation, a novel technique that decouples token interactions at different noise levels in diffusion models, revealing that the performance gains from Self-Flow over SRA primarily stem from data augmentation rather than token interactions. This method not only maintains the dual-timestep input structure of Self-Flow but also enhances training data by splitting images into multiple effective parts, leading to improved generation quality on ImageNet. The findings challenge previous assumptions about the mechanisms driving improvements in self-alignment methods.
items.judge: The paper discusses self-alignment methods in diffusion models, relevant to generative media.

items.title: OrbitQuant: Data-Agnostic Quantization for Image and Video Diffusion Transformers
items.details: Donghyun Lee, Jitesh Chavan, Duy Nguyen, Sam Huang et al. · 23 HF upvotes
items.summary: OrbitQuant introduces a novel data-agnostic quantization method for image and video diffusion transformers (DiTs), significantly improving post-training quantization (PTQ) efficiency. By utilizing a normalized, rotated basis for quantization, it eliminates the need for recalibrating data across different checkpoints and modalities, achieving state-of-the-art PTQ performance at low-bit settings (W2A4) while maintaining generation quality across multiple models, including FLUX.1 and CogVideoX.
items.judge: The paper discusses quantization techniques for image and video diffusion transformers, relevant to generative models.

items.title: DataComp-VLM: Improved Open Datasets for Vision-Language Models
items.details: Matteo Farina, Vishaal Udandarao, Thao Nguyen, Selim Kuzucu et al. · 15 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces DataComp-VLM (DCVLM), a benchmark designed for evaluating data curation strategies in Vision-Language Models (VLMs), featuring a corpus of 160 datasets and 6 trillion multimodal tokens. Key findings indicate that data mixing, particularly with instruction-heavy mixtures, significantly enhances model performance, achieving 63.6% accuracy on a 33-task core suite with 200 billion training tokens, surpassing the previous state-of-the-art dataset, FineVision, by 5.4 percentage points. All resources related to DCVLM will be publicly accessible at https://www.datacomp.ai/dcvlm/.
items.judge: The introduction of a benchmark for VLM datasets is relevant to multimodal model training.

items.title: MultAttnAttrib: Training-Free Multimodal Attribution in Long Document Question Answering
items.details: Dang Quang Thien Tran, Quang V. Dang, Vinamra Tyagi, Sai Soorya Rao Veeravalli et al. · 4 HF upvotes
items.summary: MultAttnAttrib introduces a training-free method for multimodal attribution in long document question answering, leveraging prefill passes and attention heads to identify source evidence. It outperforms existing attribution methods, including strong prompting approaches, and matches the performance of advanced models like GPT 5.4, while achieving attribution generation at up to one-seventh the latency of traditional prompting. The accompanying MultAttrEval dataset provides a novel benchmark for evaluating multimodal attributions in long-form documents.
items.judge: The method for multimodal attribution in QA systems relates to vision-language models.

items.title: Discrete Diffusion Language Models for Interactive Radiology Report Drafting
items.details: Max Van Puyvelde, Halil Ibrahim Gulluk, Wim Van Criekinge, Olivier Gevaert · 5 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces DiffusionGemma-26B, a mixture-of-experts diffusion language model that outperforms its autoregressive counterpart, Gemma-4-26B, in medical visual question answering tasks, achieving competitive results while being 3.5-4.4x faster in decoding. Notably, the diffusion model enables any-order infill, allowing radiologists to edit report fragments and have the model seamlessly generate text between them, addressing the limitations of autoregressive models in handling inconsistent medical reports.
items.judge: This work adapts a diffusion model for medical image generation, linking to generative media.

items.title: karpathy/nanoGPT
items.details: `python` · 60,840★ · 88 stars today
items.summary: The repository "karpathy/nanoGPT" provides a streamlined framework for training and fine-tuning medium-sized GPT models, emphasizing simplicity and speed. It features a minimalistic design that allows researchers to quickly implement and experiment with generative language models, making it an efficient tool for those looking to optimize training workflows without extensive overhead.
items.judge: This repository focuses on training/fine-tuning GPTs, relevant to generative models.

items.title: InstanceControl: Controllable Complex Image Generation without Instance Labeling
items.details: Xiaoyu Liu, Huan Wang, Fan Li, Zhixin Wang et al. · 8 HF upvotes
items.summary: InstanceControl introduces a novel approach to controllable multi-instance image generation that eliminates the need for labor-intensive instance labeling. By leveraging a Vision-Language Model (VLM) to establish instance-level correspondences between text prompts and visual conditions, the method enhances fidelity and control in complex scenes. The adaptive mask refinement strategy further improves the accuracy of instance masks during generation, resulting in superior performance compared to existing state-of-the-art methods.
items.judge: The paper presents a novel method for controllable image generation, relevant to generative image models.

items.title: The Mirage of Optimizing Training Policies: Monotonic Inference Policies as the Real Objective for LLM Reinforcement Learning
items.details: Jing Liang, Hongyao Tang, Yi Ma, Yancheng He et al. · 5 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces Monotonic Inference Policy Improvement (MIPI), a novel reinforcement learning (RL) objective designed to address the training-inference mismatch in large language models (LLMs), which has been a source of instability in RL training. The proposed Monotonic Inference Policy Update (MIPU) framework enhances training stability and average reasoning performance, demonstrating significant improvements in scenarios with high mismatch between training and inference policies. Experiments indicate that MIPU effectively synchronizes candidate updates, leading to more reliable deployment outcomes.

items.title: Breaking Failure Cascades: Step-Aware Reinforcement Learning for Medical Multimodal Reasoning

items.title: AnyGroundBench: A Specialized-Domain Benchmark for Video Grounding in Vision-Language Models

items.title: CogSENet: Blind Image Deblurring with Blur-Conditioned Semantic Routing and Explicit Frequency Fusion

items.title: Multi-Resolution Flow Matching: Training-Free Diffusion Acceleration via Staged Sampling
