items.title: Physics Question Scene Graph: Fine-grained Evaluation of Physical Plausibility in Text-to-Video Generation
items.details: Atin Pothiraj, Jaemin Cho, Yue Zhang, Elias Stengel-Eskin et al. · 1 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces the Physics Question Scene Graph (PQSG), a novel evaluation framework for assessing physical plausibility in text-to-video generation, which improves upon existing methods by providing a hierarchical, question-based approach that captures logical dependencies among queries. PQSG is validated using the FinePhyEval dataset, demonstrating a higher correlation with human judgments compared to prior evaluation techniques, and it effectively identifies physical law violations in generated videos from state-of-the-art models. The authors also benchmark two vision-language models, revealing that while they can generate human-like questions, their performance in answering them remains inferior to human capabilities.
items.judge: The introduction of a granular evaluation method for text-to-video generation aligns with generative video models.

items.title: UnityShots: Memory-Driven Multi-Shot Audio-Video Generation with Boundary-Aware Gating
items.details: Jiehui Huang, Yuechen Zhang, Bin Xia, Jiahao Wang et al. · 19 HF upvotes
items.summary: UnityShots introduces a memory-driven multi-shot audio-video generation system that significantly improves cross-shot coherence by utilizing a structured memory architecture with long-term and short-term memory slots, updated via a boundary-conditioned gate. Trained on a diverse dataset of 200 annotated cinematic and music-video shots, UnityShots outperforms all open-source baselines across multiple coherence metrics and matches the performance of the best closed-source systems. The system's design choices, including the injection of reference speaker tokens and a discrete cut-type prior, enhance the preservation of speaker identity and control over transition strength.
items.judge: It presents a method for multi-shot audio-video generation, relevant to generative video models.

items.title: Why Multi-Step Tool-Use Reinforcement Learning Collapses and How Supervisory Signals Fix It
items.details: Yupu Hao, Zhuoran Jin, Huanxuan Liao, Kang Liu et al. · 2 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper presents a systematic investigation into the instability of multi-step tool-use in reinforcement learning (RL) for large language models (LLMs), identifying catastrophic performance collapses due to unexpected probability spikes in control tokens. The authors propose the integration of diverse supervisory signals, particularly interleaved supervised fine-tuning with RL, which significantly enhances stability in training, although it may degrade performance on out-of-distribution evaluations. The findings underscore the necessity of understanding RL failures and leveraging supervisory signals to improve the robustness of LLMs in complex tasks.
items.judge: The paper discusses reinforcement learning techniques that could be applicable to generative media tasks.

items.title: NVIDIA-NeMo/Speech
items.details: `python` · 17,562★ · 12 stars today
items.summary: NVIDIA-NeMo/Speech is a scalable generative AI framework designed for developing Large Language Models and multimodal applications, specifically focusing on Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) and Text-to-Speech (TTS). It offers modular components that facilitate rapid experimentation and deployment, enhancing the efficiency of model training and inference. The framework supports state-of-the-art architectures and provides tools for fine-tuning and evaluation, making it a comprehensive resource for researchers in speech AI.
items.judge: This framework supports multimodal and speech AI, aligning with secondary focus on generative models.

items.title: cheahjs/free-llm-api-resources
items.details: `python` · 24,192★ · 80 stars today
items.summary: The GitHub repository "cheahjs/free-llm-api-resources" provides a curated list of free API resources for LLM inference, enabling researchers to access various models without incurring costs. Notable design choices include categorization by model type and ease of use, facilitating quick integration into existing workflows for testing and experimentation with open-source LLMs.
items.judge: This is a list of free LLM inference resources, relevant to secondary focus on open-source LLMs.

items.title: DiffusionBench: On Holistic Evaluation of Diffusion Transformers
items.details: Xingjian Leng, Jaskirat Singh, Zhanhao Liang, Ethan Smith et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: The paper introduces NanoGen, a unified training and evaluation framework for diffusion transformers (DiTs) that enables efficient training for both class-conditional generation on ImageNet and text-to-image (T2I) generation with only 12 lines of configuration changes. The authors demonstrate that training T2I models under NanoGen requires comparable computational resources to ImageNet, and they present DiffusionBench, a holistic benchmark that reveals a lack of correlation between ImageNet and T2I performance, emphasizing the need for dual-task evaluation in DiT research.
items.judge: The paper discusses evaluation metrics for diffusion transformers in image generation.

items.title: FLUX3D: High-Fidelity 3D Gaussian Generation with Diffusion-Aligned Sparse Representation
items.details: Haorui Ji, Weizhe Liu, Hongdong Li, Hengkai Guo · cs.CV cs.AI
items.summary: FLUX3D introduces a novel framework for image-to-3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) that enhances representation learning and cross-modal alignment, addressing key bottlenecks in existing methods. By employing Diffusion-Aligned Structured Latents (DA-SLAT) and a sparse-structure-aware diffusion framework, FLUX3D significantly improves the fidelity of 3DGS reconstruction, outperforming state-of-the-art methods in benchmark tests. The integration of a decoder-only architecture and advanced positional embedding techniques allows for effective alignment of dense 2D image tokens with sparse 3D voxel latents, resulting in superior visual detail preservation.
items.judge: This work focuses on high-fidelity 3D Gaussian generation, relevant to generative media.

items.title: IV-CoT: Implicit Visual Chain-of-Thought for Structure-Aware Text-to-Image Generation
items.details: Zixuan Li, Haokun Lin, Yicheng Xiao, Zhiwei Li et al. · cs.CV cs.AI
items.summary: The paper introduces Implicit Visual Chain-of-Thought (IV-CoT), a novel framework for text-to-image generation that enhances structure-aware prompt following by separating structural planning from appearance rendering. By employing a structural-to-semantic cascade and training-only sketch supervision, IV-CoT achieves superior performance on GenEval and T2I-CompBench benchmarks, demonstrating improved handling of object counts, spatial relations, and attribute bindings in generated images. This approach allows for implicit visual reasoning in a single forward pass, streamlining the generation process.
items.judge: The paper proposes a method for structure-aware text-to-image generation.

items.title: GeoT2V-Bench: Benchmarking 3D Consistency in Text-to-Video Models via 3D Reconstruction
items.details: Chenrui Fan, Paolo Favaro · cs.CV
items.summary: GeoT2V-Bench introduces a novel benchmarking framework for evaluating the 3D consistency of camera-prompted text-to-video models through a reconstruction-based approach. It provides a continuous reconstruction profile rather than a binary pass/fail, assessing metrics such as image motion, static rendering error, and flow agreement across 3,840 reconstructions from various model configurations. The findings reveal significant discrepancies in performance metrics, highlighting complementary failure modes in generated videos when treated as global static-scene captures.
items.judge: The introduction of a benchmark for evaluating 3D consistency in T2V models is highly relevant.

items.title: OrbitForge: Text-to-3D Scene Generation via Reconstruction-Anchored Video Synthesis
items.details: Chenrui Fan, Paolo Favaro · cs.CV cs.AI
items.summary: OrbitForge introduces a novel approach for text-to-3D scene generation by leveraging frozen video priors and Gaussian Splatting reconstruction, achieving a median span of 359.0 degrees and improving the Q10 ImageReward from 8.07 to 16.36 compared to traditional methods. This method enhances 3D consistency by using video reconstruction as an anchor, allowing for the completion of missing viewpoints without requiring task-specific fine-tuning or progressive view generation. The design emphasizes coverage-aware evaluation, addressing limitations in existing generative models.
items.judge: The paper presents a method for text-to-3D scene generation using video synthesis, aligning with generative media.

items.title: SER: Learning to Ground Video Reasoning with Semantic Evidence Rewards
items.details: Sheng Xia, Zhengqin Lai, Tianxiang Jiang, Kanghui Tian et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: The paper introduces Semantic Evidence Reward (SER), a novel approach for improving spatio-temporal reasoning in video MLLMs by reformulating evidence grounding as a constrained verification task. SER leverages a referee vision-language model (VLM) to assess the relevance and localization quality of generated evidence, achieving a 3.0-point improvement on the V-STAR benchmark, reaching 49.6% mLGM, while reducing dependency on dense box annotations. This method enhances both answer accuracy and evidence grounding in video question answering tasks.
items.judge: The paper proposes a new reward mechanism for video reasoning, directly related to video generation.

items.title: FLAT: Feedforward Latent Triangle Splatting for Geometrically Accurate Scene Generation
items.details: Orest Kupyn, Goutam Bhat, Philipp Henzler, Fabian Manhardt et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: The paper introduces FLAT, a novel method for generating geometrically accurate 3D scenes from compressed video diffusion latents by decoding triangle splats directly, improving upon traditional volumetric 3D Gaussians. FLAT employs a ray-centered rotation parameterization and a product window function to enhance gradient flow, achieving superior geometric accuracy while maintaining competitive visual quality on standard benchmarks. Additionally, a lightweight refinement step transforms the output into a fully opaque representation suitable for real-time rendering in game engines.
items.judge: The paper addresses 3D scene generation from images, relevant to generative models.

items.title: High-Fidelity Synthetic Transmission Electron Microscopy Image Generation Using Diffusion Probabilistic Models for Data-Limited Semiconductor Metrology
items.details: Johannes Boehm, Bappaditya Dey · cs.CV eess.IV
items.summary: The authors present a Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Model (DDPM) for generating high-fidelity synthetic Transmission Electron Microscopy (TEM) images, achieving MS-SSIM scores greater than 0.98, which indicates excellent structural similarity to real images. The model employs a progressive patch-based training strategy that allows effective training with as few as 15 samples, integrating techniques such as TrivialAugment adaptation and classifier guidance to ensure compliance with semiconductor metrology requirements. Additionally, the generated images facilitate downstream machine learning tasks like defect detection and segmentation while maintaining statistical and physical realism.
items.judge: The generation of synthetic TEM images using diffusion models is relevant to generative image models.

items.title: Multi4D: High-Fidelity Dynamic Gaussian Splatting via Multi-Level Competitive Allocation
items.details: Rui Wang, Quentin Lohmeyer, Siyu Tang, Mirko Meboldt · 0 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces Multi4D, a novel framework for high-fidelity dynamic Gaussian splatting that addresses the trade-off between motion consistency and visual fidelity by employing a multi-level competitive allocation strategy. This approach utilizes three structured levels—static structure, persistent dynamic geometry, and transient appearance primitives—to dynamically optimize photometric error, resulting in state-of-the-art rendering quality and real-time performance with significantly fewer dynamic primitives. Additionally, Multi4D achieves an order-of-magnitude speedup in 4D segmentation accuracy by embedding semantic features into its compact persistent Gaussian representation.
items.judge: The framework for high-fidelity dynamic Gaussian splatting is relevant to 3D generation.

items.title: Are Text-to-Image Models Inductivist Turkeys? A Counterfactual Benchmark for Causal Reasoning
items.details: Jiayi Lei, Yuandong Pu, Xingyu Han, Rongpeng Zhu et al. · 5 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces the Counterfactual-World (CF-World) benchmark to evaluate text-to-image (T2I) models' causal reasoning capabilities by testing their performance across three levels of counterfactual generation. Using a Vision Language Model-based evaluator (CF-Eval), the authors present two new metrics: Prior Resistance Rate (PRR) and Reasoning Retention Rate (RRR), revealing that all evaluated models significantly degrade in performance when generating counterfactual images, indicating a reliance on entrenched real-world priors rather than genuine causal understanding.
items.judge: Explores causal reasoning in text-to-image models, relevant to generative image evaluation.

items.title: Semantic Browsing: Controllable Diversity for Image Generation
items.details: Sara Dorfman, Maya Vishnevsky, Omer Dahary, Or Patashnik et al. · 10 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces "Semantic Browsing," a novel method for enhancing diversity in image generation by allowing users to navigate structured galleries of images based on meaningful semantic variations rather than incidental changes. This approach leverages rich textual representations to decouple semantic decision-making from pixel generation, resulting in a more interpretable and controlled exploration of design spaces. The authors demonstrate that their method significantly improves the diversity of generated samples while maintaining user-understandable semantic coherence.
items.judge: The method for controllable diversity in image generation is significant for generative image models.

items.title: Qwen-AgentWorld: Language World Models for General Agents
items.details: Yuxin Zuo, Zikai Xiao, Li Sheng, Fei Huang et al. · 72 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces Qwen-AgentWorld, a novel language world model comprising Qwen-AgentWorld-35B-A3B and Qwen-AgentWorld-397B-A17B, which simulates agentic environments across seven domains using long chain-of-thought reasoning. This model leverages over 10 million interaction trajectories and employs a three-stage training pipeline that enhances simulation fidelity through reinforcement learning, outperforming existing models on the newly proposed AgentWorldBench benchmark. Additionally, it serves as both a decoupled environment simulator for scalable RL training and a foundation model that improves performance on seven agentic benchmarks.
items.judge: Investigates language world models for general agents, relevant to world modeling in generative contexts.

items.title: Lift4D: Harmonizing Single-View 3D Estimation for 4D Reconstruction In-the-Wild
items.details: Yehonathan Litman, Xiaoxuan Ma, Manan Shah, Nicolas Ugrinovic et al. · 2 HF upvotes
items.summary: Lift4D introduces a test-time optimization framework for reconstructing dynamic non-rigid objects from monocular video, significantly enhancing 4D reconstruction capabilities in complex scenarios with large deformations and occlusions. By adapting a single-view 3D reconstruction model for temporally consistent predictions and employing an occlusion-aware optimization process, Lift4D improves the fidelity of surface detail recovery and unobserved region completion, outperforming previous methods on challenging datasets. This approach effectively combines direct visual observations with data-driven priors, addressing limitations in both data scarcity and reconstruction accuracy.
items.judge: This work on 4D reconstruction from monocular video relates to 3D generation techniques.

items.title: EventVLA: Event-Driven Visual Evidence Memory for Long-Horizon Vision-Language-Action Policies
items.details: Ganlin Yang, Zhangzheng Tu, Yuqiang Yang, Sitong Mao et al. · 1 HF upvotes
items.summary: EventVLA introduces a novel end-to-end framework for long-horizon Vision-Language-Action (VLA) policies, significantly enhancing memory efficiency through a sparse visual evidence memory system. By integrating foundational visual anchors and a dynamic Keyframe Evidence Memory (KEM) module, it autonomously captures and retains task-critical visual events, resulting in a 40% average success rate improvement across 17 simulation tasks and 4 real-world bimanual tasks compared to existing memory-augmented methods. The framework also includes the RoboTwin-MeM benchmark for evaluating non-Markovian manipulation tasks.
items.judge: The paper discusses vision-language-action policies, relevant to multimodal reasoning.

items.title: FlowR2A: Learning Reward-to-Action Distribution for Multimodal Driving Planning
items.details: Xirui Li, Zhe Liu, Xiaoqing Ye, Wenhua Han et al. · 1 HF upvotes
items.summary: FlowR2A introduces a novel generative model for multimodal driving planning that reconciles the benefits of scoring-based and anchor-based methods by reframing simulation-based rewards into generative conditions. It utilizes a flow-matching decoder to learn reward-conditioned action distributions from dense trajectory-reward pairs, achieving state-of-the-art performance on the NAVSIM v1 and v2 benchmarks with significantly improved proposal quality. Key innovations include fine-grained per-timestep reward conditioning and reward noise augmentation, enabling controllable test-time sampling and enhancing the model's ability to balance safety and progress objectives.
items.judge: The paper addresses multimodal driving planning, relevant to generative media through planning and action generation.

items.title: World Value Models for Robotic Manipulation
items.details: Zhihao Wang, Jianxiong Li, Yu Cui, Yuan Gao et al. · 1 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces the World Value Model (WVM), a novel approach that integrates world models with value estimation for robotic manipulation, achieving state-of-the-art Value-Order Correlation (VOC) results on standard benchmarks. WVM demonstrates robustness by maintaining performance on the newly proposed Suboptimal-Value-Bench, which includes 800 suboptimal trajectories with human-labeled annotations, and enhances manipulation performance across various policy extraction methods in both simulated and real-world settings. This advancement addresses the limitations of existing Vision-Language Model (VLM) backbones in temporal modeling, enabling better learning from mixed-quality data.
items.judge: Discusses value models in robotic manipulation, linking to world models but not generative media.

items.title: BerriAI/litellm
items.details: `python` · 51,405★ · 152 stars today
items.summary: BerriAI's litellm is a Python SDK and proxy server designed to streamline access to over 100 LLM APIs, including those from OpenAI, Azure, and HuggingFace, while providing features such as cost tracking, guardrails, load balancing, and logging. This tool enhances the integration of multiple LLMs by standardizing API calls into a unified format, improving operational efficiency for developers managing diverse LLM resources. The repository emphasizes modular design, allowing easy extension and customization for various use cases in generative modeling.
items.judge: This SDK provides access to multiple LLM APIs, relevant for multimodal applications.

items.title: Keep The Essentials: Efficient Reference Conditioned Generation via Token Dropping
items.details: Rishubh Parihar, Ayush Raina, R. Venkatesh Babu, Or Patashnik · cs.CV
items.summary: The paper introduces Sparse Context, a novel method for enhancing the efficiency of reference-based diffusion models by implementing a token dropping strategy that retains only a subset of reference tokens. This approach achieves a 4x increase in inference speed for multi-reference generation and a 2x increase for single reference generation, all while maintaining visual quality in both spatially-aligned editing and subject-driven generation. The method includes a training strategy that encourages robustness to partial reference representations and allows for task-aware token selection during inference.
items.judge: The paper presents a method for efficient reference-based image generation, aligning with generative image models.

items.title: DiT-Reward: Generative Representations for Text-to-Image Reward Modeling
items.details: Yuanming Yang, Guoqing Ma, Bo Wang, Yuan Zhang et al. · cs.LG cs.AI
items.summary: DiT-Reward introduces a novel approach to text-to-image reward modeling by transforming a pretrained text-to-image Diffusion Transformer into a reward model, achieving significant performance improvements over HPSv3, with scores of 85.6% on HPDv2 and 77.6% on HPDv3. The method leverages near-clean image latents and aggregates text-conditioned representations across transformer layers, demonstrating optimal performance in the middle-to-late layers and achieving a 1.65x inference speedup over HPSv3 while maintaining comparable peak memory usage. This work highlights the potential of pretrained generative models for enhancing reward prediction and policy optimization in generative tasks.
items.judge: The paper presents a method for text-to-image reward modeling, directly relevant to generative image models.

items.title: Vera: A Layered Diffusion Model for Content-Preserving Video Editing
items.details: Hongkai Zheng, Ta-Ying Cheng, Benjamin Klein, Yisong Yue et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: Vera introduces a novel layered diffusion framework for content-preserving video editing, addressing the challenge of unwanted alterations in static elements during video generation. By generating an edit layer and an alpha matte for compositing, Vera separates creative edits from content preservation, leveraging a Mixture-of-Transformers architecture for coherent composition. In evaluations, Vera demonstrates superior content preservation compared to leading open-source models while maintaining competitive edit quality, trained on a dataset of 486K frames with accurate alpha mattes.
items.judge: The paper presents a novel video editing framework that preserves content, directly relevant to generative video models.

items.title: Arbor: Explicit Geometric Conditioning for Controllable 3D Asset Generation
items.details: Jan-Niklas Dihlmann, Andreas Engelhardt, Simon Donne, Hendrik P. A. Lensch et al. · cs.CV cs.GR
items.summary: Arbor introduces a novel approach to 3D asset generation by integrating explicit geometric conditioning through constraint meshes, allowing for precise control over spatial occupancy and avoidance during the generation process. This method enhances constraint obedience while maintaining object quality and variation, as demonstrated through evaluations against both automatic benchmarks and user preference studies. The design choice of converting constraint meshes into tokens for a routed attachment within a frozen denoiser marks a significant advancement in controllable 3D generation.
items.judge: The paper discusses controllable 3D asset generation, aligning well with generative media topics.

items.title: MeshFlow: Mesh Generation with Equivariant Flow Matching
items.details: Qi Sun, Kiyohiro Nakayama, Jing Nathan Yan, Qixing Huang et al. · cs.GR cs.CV
items.summary: MeshFlow introduces a novel approach to directly generate triangle meshes as triangle soups using equivariant optimal-transport flow matching, which respects the symmetries of mesh representations. This method enhances the Diffusion Transformer architecture, achieving mesh quality on par with leading autoregressive models while delivering an 18× speedup in inference time. The training objective is designed to improve convergence by avoiding supervision signals that disrupt the inherent symmetries of the mesh structure.
items.judge: The paper discusses mesh generation, which is relevant to 3D generation in generative media.

items.title: Semantic Browsing: Controllable Diversity for Image Generation
items.details: Sara Dorfman, Maya Vishnevsky, Omer Dahary, Or Patashnik et al. · cs.CV cs.AI cs.GR
items.summary: The paper introduces "Semantic Browsing," a novel approach to enhance diversity in image generation by enforcing structured variation at the text level rather than relying on stochastic outputs from text-to-image models. This method allows users to navigate interpretable axes of variation, resulting in a more meaningful exploration of generated images. The authors demonstrate that their approach produces diverse outputs that correspond to specific semantic decisions, significantly improving the creative exploration capabilities of generative models.
items.judge: The paper introduces a method for controllable diversity in image generation, relevant to generative image models.

items.title: IMAGIN-4D: Image-Guided Controllable Interaction Generation
items.details: Sai Kumar Dwivedi, Federica Bogo, Buğra Tekin, Chenhongyi Yang et al. · cs.CV
items.summary: IMAGIN-4D introduces a novel diffusion-based approach for generating human-object interactions (HOI) by decomposing image conditioning into spatial and temporal components, allowing for enhanced control over interaction details. This method utilizes supervised interaction-state tokens and frame-aware visual tokens to improve the fidelity of generated motions, outperforming existing baselines in fine-grained interaction control while maintaining waypoint-following and motion quality. The authors also present a synthetic motion-to-image rendering pipeline and an image-adherence metric to evaluate the alignment of generated motions with reference images.
items.judge: The paper focuses on generating human-object interactions, which is relevant to controllable image generation.

items.title: GeoFidelity-Bench: Evaluating Segment-Level Geographic Fidelity in Text-to-Image Street-View Generation
items.details: Kaizhen Tan, Hanzhe Hong, Siru Tao · cs.CV
items.summary: GeoFidelity-Bench introduces a benchmark for evaluating segment-level geographic fidelity in text-to-image street-view generation, comprising 7,117 curated images from 109 OpenStreetMap road segments across 25 cities. The study finds that incorporating street and neighborhood names improves top-1 retrieval accuracy by 5.5 percentage points compared to city-only prompts, although the similarity to the nearest segment remains negligible, highlighting a gap between local plausibility and exact segment identity in generated outputs. This benchmark enables a more nuanced assessment of generative models' performance in producing contextually accurate street views.
items.judge: The paper introduces a benchmark for evaluating geographic fidelity in text-to-image generation.

items.title: Foresight: Failure Detection for Long-Horizon Robotic Manipulation with Action-Conditioned World Model Latents
items.details: Haoran Zhang, Yifu Lu, Boyang Wang, Xuhui Kang et al. · 8 HF upvotes
items.summary: Foresight introduces a novel failure detection framework for long-horizon robotic manipulation tasks, utilizing latent representations from an action-conditioned world model to monitor manipulation trajectories. The method, trained solely on final task-level success or failure labels, demonstrates improved reliability in failure detection compared to state-of-the-art methods, validated through evaluations on multiple simulation environments and real robotic tasks. Notably, it employs functional conformal prediction to adaptively calibrate detection thresholds, enhancing its scalability and effectiveness across different policies.
items.judge: The paper presents a framework for failure detection in robotic manipulation using world models, relevant to generative video and simulation.

items.title: Robusto-2: Benchmarking Humans & VLMs for Autonomous Driving in Lima & New York City
items.details: Adrian Cespedes, Marcelo Chincha, Dunant Cusipuma, Victor Flores-Benites et al. · 1 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces the Robusto-2 benchmark, which evaluates the performance of human drivers and Vision-Language Models (VLMs) in autonomous driving scenarios across Lima and New York City using dashcam footage. The study reveals that while both humans and VLMs exhibit divergent responses based on question types in a Visual Question Answering (VQA) framework, geographical differences do not significantly affect their answers, highlighting the challenges of out-of-distribution scenarios in autonomous driving. The dataset used for this analysis is publicly available on Hugging Face.
items.judge: The benchmarking of VLMs in autonomous driving has relevance to multi-modal reasoning.

items.title: BioMatrix: Towards a Comprehensive Biological Foundation Model Spanning the Modality Matrix of Sequences, Structures, and Language
items.details: Qizhi Pei, Zhimeng Zhou, Yi Duan, Yiyang Zhao et al. · 19 HF upvotes
items.summary: BioMatrix introduces a novel multimodal foundation model that integrates sequences, structures, and natural language for molecules and proteins within a single decoder-only architecture, achieving state-of-the-art or competitive performance on 77 out of 80 tasks across various biological applications. By employing a unified tokenization scheme, it allows for seamless consumption and generation of multiple modalities without the need for external encoders or modality-specific heads. Built on the Qwen3 model and pretrained on 304.4 billion tokens, BioMatrix effectively bridges the gap between different entity types and modalities in biological modeling.
items.judge: This paper presents a multimodal foundation model, relevant to the secondary focus on multimodal models.

items.title: Toward Open Weight Models Without Risks: Separating Public and Private Capabilities in LLMs
items.details: Charbel El Feghali, Arkil Patel, Nicholas Meade, Spandana Gella et al. · 5 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces Tiered Language Models (TLMs), which allow a single set of weights to support multiple capability levels, enhancing control over sensitive functionalities in open-weight LLMs. By employing a compact secret key to modify a subset of parameters, TLMs can exhibit additional capabilities such as new language acquisition and instruction-following, while maintaining a public configuration that prevents access to these features. The authors demonstrate this approach with 180M- and 650M-parameter models, showing that the keyed configuration can outperform the public model in specific tasks without compromising security against fine-tuning-based extraction.
items.judge: This paper discusses open-weight LLMs, relevant to the secondary focus on open-source LLM releases.

items.title: DataClaw0: Agentic Tailoring Multimodal Data from Raw Streams
items.details: Cong Wan, Zeyu Guo, Zijian Cai, Jiangyang Li et al. · 61 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces DataClaw_0, a novel approach to multimodal data processing that employs Agentic Data Tailoring to actively refine unstructured data streams, significantly improving data quality for AI training. By utilizing a two-stage pipeline that integrates generative semantic synthesis with deterministic Factual Anchors, the model achieves enhanced alignment with complex user intents, demonstrating superior performance in video generation, visual question answering, and GUI navigation. The authors also present DataClaw_0-val, a benchmark for evaluating data refinement capabilities, showcasing the model's effectiveness in adapting to new tasks with limited training data.
items.judge: The paper discusses multimodal data processing, which is relevant but not directly tied to generative image or video models.

items.title: Show HN: Shumai – open-source Frame.io alternative for creative work
items.details: 38 HN pts · [discussion](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48642686)
items.summary: Shumai is an open-source alternative to Frame.io designed for creative collaboration, enabling users to upload, review, and comment on video content in real-time. Notable design choices include a focus on user-friendly interfaces and integration with existing workflows, which enhances collaboration efficiency for teams working on video projects. The repository provides a customizable platform for managing video assets, making it suitable for both individual creators and larger teams.
items.judge: This is an open-source tool that may relate to creative work but lacks a direct tie to generative media.

items.title: SpatialAvatar-0: High-Quality 4D Head Avatar with Multi-Stage Reconstruction
items.details: Yiran Wang, Zeyu Zhang, Yuanming Li, Ziming Wang et al. · 2 HF upvotes
items.summary: SpatialAvatar-0 introduces a novel approach for generating high-quality 4D head avatars from limited source portraits, improving upon existing methods by utilizing a shared FLAME-mesh-bound Gaussian representation. This method achieves a +1.5 dB PSNR improvement over the leading GAGAvatar on cross-domain zero-shot tasks and surpasses the 300K-iteration GeoAvatar by +1.3 dB PSNR while reducing the per-subject refinement schedule by up to 60 times. Key design choices include a parameter-free K-source mean-pool and a layout-preserving refinement loop that enhances efficiency and quality without the need for extensive training on specific datasets.
items.judge: The paper discusses high-quality 4D head avatars, which is relevant to generative 3D media.

items.title: The FID Lottery: Quantifying Hidden Randomness in Generative-Model Evaluation
items.details: Nicolas Dufour, Alexei A. Efros, Patrick Pérez · 6 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper "The FID Lottery" quantifies the variability of the Frechet Inception Distance (FID) in generative model evaluation, revealing that retraining a model with a different seed can shift FID by 3.2 times more than resampling from a fixed network. The authors identify three key factors contributing to this variance and propose a new evaluation protocol that includes optimal per-cell guidance and recommends reporting error bars over multiple training seeds, rather than relying on a single FID score.
items.judge: The paper addresses the evaluation of generative models using FID, a key metric in image generation.

items.title: DF3DV-1K: A Large-Scale Dataset and Benchmark for Distractor-Free Novel View Synthesis
items.details: Cheng-You Lu, Yi-Shan Hung, Wei-Ling Chi, Hao-Ping Wang et al. · 31 HF upvotes
items.summary: The DF3DV-1K dataset introduces a large-scale benchmark for distractor-free novel view synthesis, comprising 1,048 scenes with 89,924 images that include both clean and cluttered views, addressing a significant gap in existing datasets. It facilitates the evaluation of nine recent distractor-free radiance field methods and 3D Gaussian Splatting, revealing performance improvements of 0.96 dB PSNR and 0.057 LPIPS when fine-tuning a diffusion-based 2D enhancer. This dataset aims to advance research in distractor-free vision and support broader applications beyond scene-specific reconstructions.
items.judge: Introduces a large-scale dataset for novel view synthesis, crucial for 3D generation.

items.title: JanusMesh: Fast and Zero-Shot 3D Visual Illusion Generation via Cross-Space Denoising
items.details: Siang-Ling Zhang, Huai-Hsun Cheng, Tsung-Ju Yang, Yu-Lun Liu · 19 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces JanusMesh, a novel framework for generating 3D visual illusions that operates in a fast, zero-shot manner without the need for training. By employing a cross-space dual-branch denoising process and a view-conditioned texture synthesis module, JanusMesh achieves seamless geometric fusion and high semantic recognizability, producing realistic dual-semantic 3D illusions in just 3-5 minutes, significantly improving upon the geometric integrity and efficiency of existing methods.
items.judge: Presents a framework for generating 3D visual illusions, relevant to generative image models.

items.title: HumanScale: Egocentric Human Video Can Outperform Real-Robot Data for Embodied Pretraining
items.details: Juncheng Ma, Jianxin Bi, Yufan Deng, Xuanran Zhai et al. · 8 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces a novel approach for pretraining embodied foundation models using egocentric human video, demonstrating that it can outperform traditional teleoperated real-robot data. Specifically, models pretrained on egocentric data achieved a 24% lower validation loss and significantly higher success rates (52.5% in-distribution and 90% out-of-distribution) in real-robot task execution, suggesting a scalable paradigm for model training that leverages diverse human-generated video data. This work emphasizes the potential of egocentric video as a cost-effective alternative for enhancing model performance in embodied tasks.
items.judge: Explores embodied models using video data, relevant to generative video models.

items.title: S-Agent: Spatial Tool-Use Elicits Reasoning for Spatial Intelligence
items.details: Yalun Dai, Hao Li, Shulin Tian, Runmao Yao et al. · 38 HF upvotes
items.summary: S-Agent introduces a novel spatial tool-use paradigm that enhances spatial reasoning in visual language models (VLMs) by transforming spatial perception into scene-centric understanding through spatio-temporal evidence accumulation. This approach integrates a hierarchy of spatial tools and a temporal memory mechanism, enabling improved reasoning across continuous multi-view images and videos. Experimental results demonstrate that S-Agent outperforms both open-source and closed-source VLMs without requiring additional training, with the compact S-Agent-8B model achieving significant performance gains over similar-scale baselines and rivaling advanced closed-source models.
items.judge: Discusses spatial reasoning in a continuous 3D context, relevant to multimodal models.

items.title: lyogavin/airllm
items.details: 20,955★ · 453 stars today
items.summary: AirLLM is an open-source model that enables 70 billion parameter LLM inference on a single 4GB GPU, significantly reducing the hardware requirements for running large language models. This implementation leverages efficient quantization techniques and optimized memory management to achieve this capability, making it accessible for researchers and developers with limited resources. The repository includes detailed instructions for setup and usage, emphasizing its practical applications in resource-constrained environments.
items.judge: This item discusses an open-source LLM with notable inference capabilities.

items.title: immich-app/immich
items.details: `typescript` · 104,160★ · 144 stars today
items.summary: The Immich project is a high-performance self-hosted solution for managing photos and videos, designed to optimize storage and retrieval efficiency. It offers features such as automatic media organization and sharing capabilities, making it suitable for users seeking control over their media assets without relying on third-party services. Notable design choices include a focus on scalability and user privacy, allowing for seamless integration into personal or small business environments.
items.judge: This is a photo and video management solution, relevant to generative media.

items.title: Moebius: 0.2B image inpainting model with 10B-level performance
items.details: 89 HN pts · [discussion](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48630171)
items.summary: Moebius is a new image inpainting model that leverages a 0.2 billion parameter architecture to achieve performance comparable to 10 billion parameter models. This improvement is significant as it demonstrates that smaller models can effectively match the quality of larger counterparts, potentially reducing computational costs and resource requirements for image inpainting tasks. The model's design emphasizes efficiency while maintaining high fidelity in generated outputs.
items.judge: This model focuses on image inpainting, relevant to generative image tasks.

items.title: DF3DV-1K: A Large-Scale Dataset and Benchmark for Distractor-Free Novel View Synthesis
items.details: Cheng-You Lu, Yi-Shan Hung, Wei-Ling Chi, Hao-Ping Wang et al. · 30 HF upvotes
items.summary: The DF3DV-1K dataset introduces a large-scale benchmark for distractor-free novel view synthesis, comprising 1,048 scenes with 89,924 images that include both clean and cluttered views, addressing the lack of comprehensive datasets in this area. It enables the evaluation of nine recent distractor-free radiance field methods and 3D Gaussian Splatting, revealing the most robust techniques and challenging scenarios, while also demonstrating a fine-tuning application that yields average improvements of 0.96 dB PSNR and 0.057 LPIPS on benchmarked datasets. The dataset and leaderboard are publicly accessible, promoting advancements in distractor-free vision.
items.judge: Introduces a large-scale dataset for novel view synthesis, directly supporting generative media research.

items.title: JanusMesh: Fast and Zero-Shot 3D Visual Illusion Generation via Cross-Space Denoising
items.details: Siang-Ling Zhang, Huai-Hsun Cheng, Tsung-Ju Yang, Yu-Lun Liu · 19 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper presents JanusMesh, a novel framework for generating 3D visual illusions in a fast and training-free manner, achieving results in just 3-5 minutes. It introduces a cross-space dual-branch denoising process for voxel space decoding and Signed Distance Field blending, ensuring seamless geometric fusion, alongside a view-conditioned texture synthesis module for realistic dual-semantic outputs. JanusMesh significantly improves geometric integrity and semantic recognizability compared to existing methods, addressing issues of slow optimization and unnatural seams.
items.judge: Presents a framework for generating 3D visual illusions, contributing to 3D generation techniques.

items.title: Holo-World: Unified Camera, Object and Weather Control for Video World Model
items.details: Xiangchen Yin, Wenzhang Sun, Jiahui Yuan, Zijie Liu et al. · 6 HF upvotes
items.summary: Holo-World introduces a novel unified controllable video world model that enables simultaneous manipulation of camera, object motion, and weather conditions from a single image, significantly enhancing the fidelity of generated videos. By leveraging the HoloStateData dataset and employing a Scene-Weather Decomposed CFG, the model effectively separates world preservation and weather transfer, outperforming existing video-to-video weather editing baselines in both quantitative and qualitative assessments. This approach allows for precise control over scene structure while achieving diverse weather state transformations.
items.judge: This work on video world models with controllable elements is highly relevant to generative video.

items.title: The FID Lottery: Quantifying Hidden Randomness in Generative-Model Evaluation
items.details: Nicolas Dufour, Alexei A. Efros, Patrick Pérez · 4 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper "The FID Lottery" quantifies the variability in Frechet Inception Distance (FID) scores due to randomness in model training and sampling, revealing that retraining a model with a different seed can shift FID scores by 3.2 times more than resampling from a fixed model. The authors identify three key factors contributing to this variance and propose a new evaluation protocol that includes optimal guidance tuning and recommends reporting error bars over multiple training seeds, rather than relying on a single FID score.
items.judge: The paper evaluates FID in generative models, relevant to image generation metrics.

items.title: BrainG3N: A Dual-Purpose Tokenizer for Controllable 3D Brain MRI Generation
items.details: Max Van Puyvelde, Ibrahim Gulluk, Wim Van Criekinge, Olivier Gevaert · 2 HF upvotes
items.summary: The paper introduces BrainG3N, a novel dual-purpose tokenizer for 3D brain MRI generation that utilizes a volumetric masked-autoencoder (MAE) to decouple the encoding and decoding processes. This approach allows the encoder to produce clinically informative embeddings while a dedicated CNN decoder reconstructs anatomically accurate volumes, achieving state-of-the-art performance on 21 out of 23 tasks in a linear-probing benchmark. Additionally, a conditional diffusion transformer (DiT) trained on these embeddings enables controllable generation and patient-specific longitudinal forecasting, demonstrating the tokenizer's utility in both clinical applications and generative modeling.
items.judge: The paper discusses a tokenizer for 3D brain MRI generation, relevant to generative models in medical imaging.

items.title: PerceptionDLM: Parallel Region Perception with Multimodal Diffusion Language Models
items.details: Yueyi Sun, Yuhao Wang, Jason Li, Ye Tian et al. · 31 HF upvotes
items.summary: PerceptionDLM introduces a multimodal diffusion language model designed for efficient parallel region perception, achieving state-of-the-art performance among open-source diffusion MLLMs. By employing efficient prompting and structured attention masking, it allows simultaneous captioning of multiple regions, significantly improving inference speed compared to sequential methods. The model is evaluated using the new Parallel Detailed Localized Captioning Benchmark (ParaDLC-Bench), demonstrating competitive caption quality alongside enhanced efficiency for multi-region tasks.
items.judge: The paper presents a multimodal diffusion language model for efficient perception tasks, relevant to generative models.

items.title: HumanScale: Egocentric Human Video Can Outperform Real-Robot Data for Embodied Pretraining
items.details: Juncheng Ma, Jianxin Bi, Yufan Deng, Xuanran Zhai et al. · 7 HF upvotes
items.summary: The study introduces a novel approach for pretraining embodied foundation models using egocentric human video, demonstrating that this data source can outperform traditional teleoperated real-robot trajectories. Specifically, models pretrained on egocentric data achieved a 24% lower validation loss and 52.5% and 90% higher success rates on in-distribution and out-of-distribution real-robot tasks, respectively. This research suggests a scalable paradigm for model training, advocating for the use of egocentric video to enhance world representation learning before fine-tuning with limited real-robot data.
items.judge: Explores the use of egocentric human video for embodied pretraining, relevant to video generation.

items.title: FlowBender: Feedback-Aware Training for Self-Correcting Conditional Flows
items.details: Daniel Gilo, Sven Elflein, Ido Sobol, Or Litany · 19 HF upvotes
items.summary: FlowBender introduces a novel closed-loop framework for training conditional diffusion and flow models that directly incorporates inference-time feedback to correct alignment errors, significantly improving fidelity and plausibility in generated outputs. The method outperforms standard supervised baselines and state-of-the-art guidance techniques across tasks such as image-to-image translation and 3D mesh texturing, demonstrating enhanced performance without compromising on either fidelity or plausibility. Notably, FlowBender includes efficient sampling strategies, such as a prior-step shortcut, to minimize additional computational costs during closed-loop correction.
items.judge: The paper addresses conditional diffusion and flow models, which can relate to generative media techniques.

items.title: calesthio/OpenMontage
items.details: 9,319★ · 987 stars today
items.summary: OpenMontage is the first open-source, agentic video production system, featuring 12 pipelines, 52 tools, and over 500 agent skills designed to facilitate comprehensive video creation. This system allows users to leverage AI coding assistants to automate and enhance the video production process, significantly streamlining workflows in generative video modeling. The modular design promotes flexibility and customization for various production needs.
items.judge: This open-source video production system is directly related to generative video applications.

items.title: THUDM/slime
items.details: `python` · 6,633★ · 91 stars today
items.summary: THUDM/slime is a post-training framework designed for reinforcement learning (RL) scaling of large language models (LLMs). It enhances the efficiency of RL fine-tuning by providing a modular architecture that allows for easy integration of various RL algorithms and training strategies. The framework aims to improve the performance of LLMs in specific tasks by leveraging RL techniques, although quantitative improvements are not specified in the repository.
items.judge: This framework for RL scaling may have applications in generative media.
